helpers.name: esde_running
helpers.behavior: Returns True iff ES-DE is up. Body is exactly the existing inline check from steam-collection-sync.py:83: `return subprocess.run(['pgrep','-f','ES-DE|emulationstation'], capture_output=True).returncode == 0`. NOTE the regex is intentionally 'ES-DE|emulationstation' (matches both the AppImage process name 'ES-DE' and the legacy 'emulationstation' binary) — do NOT 'simplify' it. Pair it with a thin helper `def abort_if_esde_running(action='write the gamelist') -> bool` that prints the standard message ('ES-DE is running — close it first (it rewrites gamelists on exit). Aborting.') and returns True when it aborted, so each caller is one line: `if abort_if_esde_running(): return 1`. An equivalent inline check already exists ONLY in steam-collection-sync.py:83 — factor THAT into this module and have steam-collection-sync.py import it too (replaces_existing semantics for that one call site).

helpers.name: process_running
helpers.behavior: Generalized pgrep -f wrapper used by esde_running() and by the emulator-restore guard. `return subprocess.run(['pgrep','-f',pattern], capture_output=True).returncode == 0`. esde_running() becomes `return process_running('ES-DE|emulationstation')`. Mirrors dolphin-wii-mode.sh:32's `pgrep -fa` guard but in Python. Used by mad_backup.do_restore to refuse a restore while cemu/pcsx2/rpcs3/xemu/eden (or ES-DE) is open.

helpers.name: atomic_write_text
helpers.signature: def atomic_write_text(target: Path, content: str, *, encoding: str = 'utf-8', backup_once_suffix: str | None = None) -> None
helpers.behavior: Write `content` to a sibling temp file in the SAME directory (`target.with_suffix(target.suffix + '.tmp')`), then `os.replace(tmp, target)` so the live file is only ever swapped atomically and can never be left truncated. On any OSError, unlink the tmp and re-raise (mirrors localpolicy.dump:88-98). target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) first (mirrors retroarch_cfg._atomic_write:214). Optional `backup_once_suffix` (e.g. '.router-backup'): if given and `target` exists and the backup does not yet exist, shutil.copy2 it once before the swap (folds in the supermodel/sinden 'one-time backup' ask). This is the canonical version of THREE existing copies: retroarch_cfg._atomic_write:213, localpolicy.dump:88-98, sinden_cfg line 127-129. Provide a bytes sibling `atomic_write_bytes(target, data, ...)` for image/binary callers (steam-fetch-media). Keep the existing three as thin wrappers calling this (or leave them; they are already correct) — the NEW callers below are the point.

helpers.name: atomic_write_json
helpers.signature: def atomic_write_json(path: Path, data, *, indent: int = 2) -> None
helpers.behavior: Thin convenience: `atomic_write_text(path, json.dumps(data, indent=indent))`. Exists so tester_cmds._write_json and the Tk testers' JSON saves route through one place. REPLACES the non-atomic body of tester_cmds._write_json:228-230 (`path.write_text(json.dumps(...))`) — change that function's last line to call this. mad_gamepad_tester.py / mad_xarcade_tester.py JSON saves should call it too.

helpers.name: recoverable_delete
helpers.signature: def recoverable_delete(paths: list[Path] | Path, *, tmp_base: Path, tag: str, recovery_note: str) -> Path
helpers.behavior: Project rule #5 'never delete — move to recoverable _TMP'. Creates `tmp_base / f'_TMP_{tag}-{time.strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S")}'`, shutil.move() each given path into it, and writes/append a RECOVERY.txt containing recovery_note + a per-file manifest. Returns the _TMP dir Path so the caller can PRINT it (rule #5 requires reporting the actual path). `tmp_base` MUST be chosen same-filesystem as the files (instant move): for SD-card media use Path('/run/media/deck/1tbDeck') (matches steam-collection-sync TMP_BASE:27); for /home files use Path.home()/'Downloads'/'_TMP'. This canonicalizes the pattern that today exists ONLY inline in steam-collection-sync.py:141-155 and as ad-hoc shell in deck-fetch-esde.sh/sinden-install.sh. CALLERS pass the right tmp_base for their file's filesystem.
used_by: C0.3 (clean-manual-cruft collision — already uses _TMP, just needs the collision branch)

helpers.name: atomic_replace_artwork
helpers.signature: def atomic_replace_artwork(dst_dir: Path, stem: str, src_path: Path) -> Path
helpers.behavior: Fixes the unlink-before-copy artwork races (steam-fetch-media.place:93-97, steam-fetch-metadata fix_cover). Copy src to `dst_dir/(stem + '.router-tmp' + suffix)`, then os.replace onto the final `dst_dir/(stem+suffix)`, and ONLY AFTER the replace succeeds, unlink the OTHER differently-suffixed `glob(stem + '.*')` siblings. So an interruption never leaves the game with no artwork. Returns the final path.

helpers.name: guard_block_input_on_focus_change
helpers.location: es-app/src/main.cpp (edit existing block at 547-563 — NOT a new function; C++ fix)
helpers.signature: (in-place edit; track `static bool sFocusBlocked` and only setBlockInput on transition)
helpers.behavior: For W7.1: stop the UNCONDITIONAL per-frame `window->setBlockInput(!esHasFocus)` at main.cpp:549. Move the setBlockInput into the existing `if (esHasFocus != sHadFocus)` transition branch (lines 555-563) so the focus logic only WRITES the block flag when focus actually changes, leaving ViewController's intentional launch/rescan input-blocks intact between transitions. This reuses the transition-detection (`sHadFocus`) that the video-pause code at 555 already established — no new state machine needed beyond reusing that bool.

helpers.name: ViewController null-guard (startViewVideos/stopViewVideos/pauseViewVideos/muteViewVideos)
helpers.signature: void startViewVideos() override { if (mCurrentView != nullptr) mCurrentView->startViewVideos(); } (and the 3 siblings)
helpers.behavior: For W7.0 + W6.0: add `if (mCurrentView != nullptr)` inside the four single-line video overrides at ViewController.h:87-90, EXACTLY mirroring the sibling resetViewVideosTimer() at :91-95 which already guards the same member. This makes every external caller (including our new main.cpp focus handler that calls start/pauseViewVideos) null-safe in the no-games/no-ROMs/invalid-systems state. This is the file's own established convention — reuse it, do not invent a call-site guard.

helpers.notes:
SCOPE NOTE: This is a 5-HIGH + 28-MED + 49-LOW + leads review. I read the actual code at every finding I designed a concrete fix for. Below: (A) the shared-helper plan and where it lives, (B) concrete per-finding fixes grouped by helper, (C) honesty flags where the finding/its suggested fix is wrong/incomplete or is an unverified lead, (D) risk + verify + gates per group.

=== A. SHARED HELPER HOMES (decided after reading lib/) ===
lib/ is the shared-module home; top-level scripts already do `sys.path.insert(0, launchers_dir)` then `from lib import X` (confirmed: steam-fetch-metadata, steam-fetch-media, controller-router, etc). madsrv/*.py use relative imports (`from .. import sinden_cfg`).
Create TWO new tiny modules:
  • lib/proc_guard.py  — esde_running(), process_running(pattern), abort_if_esde_running(action).
  • lib/fsutil.py      — atomic_write_text/bytes, atomic_write_json, recoverable_delete, atomic_replace_artwork.
Rationale for two modules not one: process-guards (subprocess/pgrep) and filesystem-ops are orthogonal concerns; keeps imports honest. Both are stdlib-only (subprocess, os, shutil, time, json, pathlib) so they load fine in the bare SteamOS python the daemon and scripts use. madsrv callers import as `from .. import fsutil, proc_guard`; top-level scripts `from lib import fsutil, proc_guard`.
There is NO existing equivalent module today — only inline copies: ES-DE guard inline at steam-collection-sync.py:83; atomic write reimplemented 3x (retroarch_cfg._atomic_write:213, localpolicy.dump:88-98, sinden_cfg:127-129, plus cemu_cfg.py's own .tmp at sinden); recoverable-delete inline at steam-collection-sync.py:141-155. The helpers CONSOLIDATE these. I would NOT rip out the 3 existing atomic writers in the same change (they are already correct and the risk/value is poor) — point new callers at fsutil and optionally migrate the old ones later.

=== B. CONCRETE FIXES BY GROUP ===

[ES-DE-RUNNING GUARD — N7.0, 10.5/9.1, 11.1/9.3, 11.2/9.4, 9.2]  (note 10.5 and 9.1 are the SAME bug double-listed; 11.1/9.3 same; 11.2/9.4 same — five scripts, not nine)
Add at the very top of each script's main()/entry, before the first gamelist write, after the dry-run check:
  steam-collection-gen.py:171 (N7.0): add `from lib import proc_guard` (needs sys.path.insert like its siblings — it currently has none, ADD it) + `if proc_guard.abort_if_esde_running(): return/sys.exit(1)` before GAMELIST.write_text, AND route that write through fsutil.atomic_write_text.
  steam-fetch-metadata.py:215 (10.5/9.1): it already imports lib (sgdb). Add guard at top of main() guarded by `if not dry:`.
  dedup-disc-gamelists.py (11.1/9.3): no lib import today — add sys.path.insert + import; guard before the dedup() write at line 68.
  skyscraper-apply.py (11.2/9.4): top-level script, add sys.path.insert + import; guard before the first open(real,'w') at line 41.
  openbor-gen-gamelist.py:139 (9.2): add guard + a timestamped .bak before overwrite (it has NEITHER guard NOR backup today — worst of the set).

[ATOMIC CONFIG/GAMELIST WRITES — 10.7, 5.0, 8.1, 8.2, 10.6, 3.0]  (5.0 and 10.7 are the SAME five-backend bug double-listed)
Replace bare `.write_text()`/`open(...,'w')`/`yaml.safe_dump(f,...)` truncate-in-place with fsutil.atomic_write_text (or atomic_write_json):
  • lib/cemu_cfg.py:132,203 ; lib/pcsx2_cfg.py:195 ; lib/xemu_cfg.py:110 ; lib/eden_cfg.py:187 ; lib/rpcs3_cfg.py:183 — each already takes a one-time .router-backup just above the write; keep it. For rpcs3 the safe_dump-to-open-file is the worst (a dump exception truncates) — serialize to a string first (`text = yaml.safe_dump(data,...)`) then atomic_write_text. These 5 already have the backup escape hatch so atomicity is the only gap.
  • supermodel-sinden-smart.py:191 (8.1): no backup today — use atomic_write_text with backup_once_suffix='.router-backup' (gets both fixes the finding asked for).
  • sinden-update-retroarch-mouseindex.py:43 (8.2): atomic_write_text. ALSO handle the missing-key case: the finding correctly notes that if `input_player[12]_mouse_index` lines are absent the re.sub is a silent no-op (lightgun aim silently broken). Append the keys when `new == text` and the pattern didn't match — but this is a BEHAVIOR ADD, flag for the user.
  • lib/madsrv/tester_cmds.py:228-230 _write_json (3.0): point at fsutil.atomic_write_json.
  • lib/madsrv/sinden_cmds.py:203 + router-config-gui.py:2130 (N7.1): sinden.conf LED toggle — atomic_write_text (sinden_cfg already has the tmp+os.replace pattern at :127, so import/reuse it or fsutil).

[INJECTION — 12.0 / 5.1 cemu_cfg.py:131]  (SAME bug, double-listed)
`text = _DISPLAY_RE.sub(rf"\g<1>{dev.name}\g<3>", text, count=1)` — dev.name is interpolated into a re.sub REPLACEMENT template where `\` and `\g<n>` are special; a pad name with backslash corrupts/loses the </display_name> tag. Two-part fix: (1) XML-escape the name (`from xml.sax.saxutils import escape`); (2) use a FUNCTION replacement so the value is literal: `text = _DISPLAY_RE.sub(lambda m: m.group(1) + escape(dev.name) + m.group(3), text, count=1)`. Apply the same to the _UUID_RE.sub at :130 if uuid can ever contain backslashes (it's `{sdl_index}_{guid}` — guid is hex, low risk, but the function-replacement form is free safety). Then atomic-write per above.

[RECOVERABLE DELETE — 11.4/12.3/9.5, 6.1]  (11.4, 12.3, 9.5 are the SAME steam cover-delete bug triple-listed)
  • steam-fetch-metadata.py fix_cover (138,145): two `e.unlink()` sites. Line 138 deletes the OLD cover only AFTER a verified-better portrait was downloaded (`tmp` validated portrait) and immediately replaces it — that one is arguably fine (replace-with-better) but to honor rule #5 strictly route the old file through recoverable_delete first. Line 145 is the real data-loss: deletes the landscape cover when NO portrait exists anywhere → game left with no cover. Fix per finding: either (a) leave the landscape cover (a sideways cover beats no cover) — simplest and safest, OR (b) recoverable_delete([existing], tmp_base=Path('/run/media/deck/1tbDeck'), tag='steam-cover', recovery_note=...). I recommend (a)+log, because the cover may be user-hand-picked; flag the choice for the user.
  • lib/mad_backup.py:155-159 reset_local (6.1): `LOCAL.unlink()` → recoverable_delete([LOCAL], tmp_base=Path.home()/'Downloads'/'_TMP', tag='mad-reset', recovery_note='controller-policy.local.toml GUI overrides; move back to restore'). Return the _TMP path in the status string.

[ARTWORK RACE — 11.3, N7.2]  steam-fetch-media.place:93-97 and cdn_download/sgdb_download unlink-before-copy → route through fsutil.atomic_replace_artwork.

[QUIT-COMBO SELF-SIGTERM — 0.0]  (CONFIRMED real, reproduced)
quit-combo-watcher.py:120 runs `subprocess.run(quit_cmd, shell=True)` synchronously; the quit_cmd's own `pkill -TERM -f 'Eden|Yuzu|Suyu|Ryujinx'` matches the watcher's OWN argv, SIGTERMing the watcher before its quit_cmd's `sleep 2; pkill -KILL` second clause runs → Eden survives to the 6s global backstop instead of the intended 2s. Fix: run the quit_cmd DETACHED so it survives the watcher dying: `subprocess.Popen(['setsid','bash','-c',quit_cmd], stdout/stderr=DEVNULL)` then `return` from the watcher immediately (the backstop is already detached at :113-117). The watcher exiting is fine — its job is done. RISK: the watcher previously blocked on subprocess.run; making it Popen+return changes nothing user-visible (main() returns right after _quit anyway). VERIFY: headless — launch watcher with `--quit-cmd "echo TERM; sleep 2; echo KILL"` and a fake long-running 'Eden' process whose name matches; confirm both TERM and KILL fire and the emulator dies at ~2s not 6s. The finding's suggested fix (setsid the quit_cmd) is CORRECT.

[CAMERA/CLEANUP LEAKS — 4.0, N0.1, N1.0, N1.1, 4.2, N2.0]
  • 4.0 + N0.1: router-config-gui.py quit():1870-1875 calls _gp_cleanup + _cam_restore_driver but NOT _cam_kill_ffmpeg and NOT the Daphne _dp_proc.kill(). Cleanest fix the finding suggests: call self._clear() at the TOP of quit() so EVERY page's teardown runs (camera ffmpeg, Daphne capture, gamepad ungrab) — _clear already does all of it (read at 1340-1380). RISK: _clear touches a lot of per-page state; must confirm it's safe to call when no page-specific widgets exist (it uses getattr(...,None) guards throughout, so it looks safe) — VERIFY on-device by quitting from each page. If calling _clear() is too broad, the narrower fix is to add `_cam_kill_ffmpeg()` before `_cam_restore_driver()` and kill `_dp_proc` in quit().
  • N1.0: _cam_tick:2476-2490 only checks `if not self._cam_proc` (truthiness, never poll()) — a dead ffmpeg shows a frozen 'live' frame + zombie. Add `if self._cam_proc.poll() is not None:` early-return + status update + the poll() reaps the zombie. Suggested fix is correct.
  • 4.2: _cam ffmpeg stderr fd leaked — open the log with a `with` context manager (the finding's fix is right; trivial).
  • N1.1: 4 synchronous v4l2-ctl + sinden-stop run on the Tk UI thread → freeze. Move to a worker thread posting via self._ui_q. This is a LARGER GUI refactor — flag as medium effort, gate on on-device test.
  • N2.0: SIGTERM from ES-DE bypasses quit()/_clear cleanup. Install signal.signal(SIGTERM/SIGINT) + root.protocol('WM_DELETE_WINDOW', self.quit) routing through the same cleanup. RISK: Tk + signals need the handler to schedule via root.after (can't safely destroy Tk from a signal handler) — implement carefully; gate on on-device test.

[ROBUSTNESS — 0.1/12.4, 0.4, 12.5, 8.0, N3.0, N3.1, N3.2]
  • 0.1/12.4 (SAME bug): routing.py:48-50 base parse unguarded while local (51-56) is guarded. Wrap base in `try/except (tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, OSError): base = {'systems': {}}` + log to stderr. Finding's title 'aborts EVERY game launch' is OVERSTATED (the refuter notes this) — it aborts routed launches with a generic ES-DE error; still worth fixing. Also fixes C1.2 (the SAME unguarded base parse in lib/policy.py:21-22, a DIFFERENT file — both need the guard; C1.2/12.4/0.1 are the same class across routing.py AND policy.py).
  • 0.4: quit-combo-watcher button int() unguarded — wrap in try/except, fall back to DEFAULT_BUTTONS, skip non-numeric. Trivial, correct.
  • 12.5: openbor-fetch-media.py:72 `.group(1)` on a possibly-None re.search → guard `dm = re.search(...); dirn = dm.group(1) if dm else ''` and skip when empty. Correct.
  • 8.0 (HIGH, but see HONESTY): model-2-emulator.sh:27,30 sed targets literal "M2CONFIGFILE" not $M2CONFIGFILE — provably dead. BUT this file is the EMUDECK-DEPLOYED copy (EmuDeck overwrites it on update — confirmed in emudeckSetup.log:3724) and ES-DE actually wires model2-m2emu.sh (es_systems.xml:8), NOT this file. So fixing model-2-emulator.sh is (a) not load-bearing for our ES-DE launches and (b) will be clobbered by EmuDeck. The crosshair is ALREADY handled correctly in model2-m2emu.sh:27-30 (`"$INI"`). RECOMMENDATION: do NOT 'fix' the EmuDeck file as ours; treat 8.0 as confirming the live path is model2-m2emu.sh. The on-device checklist already flags this. This downgrades 8.0 from a HIGH bug-to-fix to dead-code-in-a-vendor-file.
  • N3.0: model2-m2emu.sh:28-29 unguarded `sed -i` on a possibly-missing EMULATOR.INI (with `set -eu`, a missing INI aborts launch). The INI EXISTS on this deck (verified). Fix: guard `[ -f "$INI" ] && case ... esac`. Correct, cheap.
  • N3.1: supermodel-sinden.sh:11-15 — exit code swallowed by `tee` (it's #!/bin/sh, no pipefail). Fix: switch shebang to bash + `exit "${PIPESTATUS[0]}"` (mirrors hypseus-pin.sh:1,84 which is already #!/usr/bin/env bash). Correct.
  • N3.2: supermodel.sh `param="${@}"` collapses argv — use `"$@"` directly. Correct.

[CONCURRENCY — 0.2/10.11, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3]  (CONTESTED — read the seam conclusion carefully)
  • 0.2/10.11 (SAME _ENUM_CACHE lock, double-listed): the SEAM CONCLUSION marks these DEAD — all RPC handlers run serially on the single stdin-reader thread; the cache is mutated synchronously there. devices.py already has `_SDL_LOCK = threading.Lock()` at :394 as precedent. The fix (add a module lock around enumerate_devices cache R/W) is cheap and harmless, but the finding's PREMISE (concurrent access) was REFUTED by the cross-wave seam analysis. VERIFY FIRST: confirm no slow=True handler calls enumerate_devices on the worker pool. If genuinely single-threaded, this is defensive-only — low priority.
  • 2.1 (set_ra_option RMW): the seam conclusion says this stays dead ONLY IF the RMW runs on the stdin thread, but systems_cmds.py:242 is `@method(slow=True)` → runs on the 4-worker ThreadPool (rpc.py:24) → two can overlap → CONFIRMED race. Fix per finding: make set_ra_option fast (drop slow=True so it serializes on the stdin thread — the write is tiny) OR add a module lock. The 'make it fast' option is simplest and matches model2.set/profiles.apply_slot. VERIFY by reading rpc.py dispatch to confirm slow=True→pool (the seam analysis already did). This one is a real fix.

[BACKUP/RESTORE GUARDS — 11.5, 6.5, 6.6]
  • 11.5: mad_backup.do_restore:102-130 copies configs back with no emulator-running check (the 'close emulators' text is only in the AFTER message:130). Add proc_guard.process_running check for the relevant emulators+ES-DE at the TOP and refuse/return an error message, mirroring dolphin-wii-mode.sh:32. Needs the list of emulator process patterns from backup_targets — derive per target.
  • 6.5: do_restore copytree(dirs_exist_ok=True) MERGES dir targets instead of replacing → resurrects stale files. Fix: for dir targets, recoverable_delete the dest first (never rm) then copytree, OR document the merge. Flag: this is a behavior decision for the user.
  • 6.6: deck-restore.sh:56-68 extracts config archive over $HOME with no ES-DE check — SHELL script, so add a `pgrep -f 'ES-DE|emulationstation'` guard inline (can't import the Python helper) mirroring dolphin-wii-mode.sh:32. The proc_guard helper is Python-only; shell callers (6.6, 6.2, C0.x) need the inline shell idiom — note this so nobody tries to import Python from bash.

=== C. HONESTY FLAGS (finding wrong / incomplete / unverified) ===
1. 8.0 (HIGH): real typo, but it's in EmuDeck's vendor file that EmuDeck overwrites and that ES-DE does NOT launch. 'Fixing' it is wasted/clobbered work. The live path (model2-m2emu.sh) is already correct. Downgrade to dead-code observation. Needs on-device confirm that nothing local sources model-2-emulator.sh (the checklist agrees).
2. 0.1/12.4 title 'aborts EVERY game launch' is overstated (refuter-confirmed): only routed launches, with a generic error. Fix still valid.
3. 0.2/10.11 (concurrency): REFUTED by the cross-wave seam conclusion (single stdin-reader thread, single client). The lock is defensive-only — VERIFY the no-pool claim before spending effort; do not present as a live race.
4. 2.1 is the ONE concurrency finding the seam conclusion REVIVES (slow=True→pool). Genuine. The others (0.2/10.x hotplug) stay dead.
5. ALL the C-prefixed and CC-prefixed items (C0.0–C1.5, CC.0–CC.8) and N4.x are completeness-critic LEADS, NOT verified findings — the doc says they were 'NOT run back through the per-finding adversarial verifier.' In particular C0.0/C1.0/C1.1 (deck-post-update samba/pacman on read-only root) are HIGH leads worth fixing (sudo + steamos-readonly disable/enable, mirroring install.sh:146-151) but each needs on-device confirmation after a real SteamOS update (the checklist lists this). CC.2 merely corroborates W4.0 (same fix). Treat all C/CC as 'verify first.'
6. The CONTESTED block (10.0 threadpool-no-shutdown, 10.3 dialog-defaults-cancel, 6.4 splash.svg-delete, etc.) is explicitly 'needs-judgment' — no fix designed; surface to the user for a decision rather than auto-fixing.
7. steam-fetch-metadata fix_cover line 138 (the replace-with-verified-better delete) is arguably NOT data loss (it just swapped in a confirmed-better portrait). Only line 145 (delete with no replacement) is the real rule-#5 violation. Don't over-fix 138.
8. None of these were reproduced on a running Deck with a display (the review says so explicitly). Every GUI/crash/in-game fix (W4.0, W6.0, W7.0, W7.1, 4.0, N0.0, N1.0, N2.0, 8.0 in-game effect) GATES on on-device verification by the user.

=== D. C++ FIXES (gate on a rebuild from ~/esde-build → ~/Applications/ES-DE-MAD.AppImage) ===
  • W7.0 + W6.0 (SAME null-deref, two reachability paths): add `if (mCurrentView != nullptr)` to the 4 video overrides in ViewController.h:87-90, mirroring resetViewVideosTimer:91-95. Confirmed-safe pattern reuse.
  • W4.0 (+CC.2 corroboration): GuiMadPageSplash.cpp:181-209 — replace the raw `SwitchComponent* sc {switchComp.get()}` capture with `std::weak_ptr<SwitchComponent> weakSc {switchComp};` and in the response callback `auto sc {weakSc.lock()}; if (!sc) return;` before any setState. This MIRRORS GuiMadPageBackends.cpp:510 (weakChips) and GuiMadPageDaphne.cpp:230 (weakSeek) which already do exactly this with explanatory comments. The page does NOT keep its switches in a member vector (unlike Systems' mToggles), so the weak_ptr approach is the right one (option b in the finding), not lookup-by-name. NOTE the existing comment at :177-180 ('capturing the shared_ptr would self-cycle') stays TRUE — a weak_ptr does NOT create the cycle, so the comment should be updated to say 'weak_ptr avoids both the dangle and the cycle.'
  • W7.1: main.cpp:549 — move setBlockInput into the existing transition branch (don't write every frame); see helper note. RISK: must ensure block is correctly ASSERTED on focus-loss and CLEARED on focus-regain at the transition, and that ViewController's own blocks aren't fought — the finding suggests OR-ing focus state with the existing block rather than overwriting, which is the more robust form. GATE: rebuild + on-device (launch-screen countdown should no longer be dismissable by a stray press, and overlay-up should still block nav).
  • C++ LOW/INFO (W0.0–W6.2, W1.x, W2.0, W4.1, W5.x, W8.x, CC.x): all gate on rebuild; most are one-line guards (clamp bounds W4.1, IsString() W2.0, stoi try/catch W6.2/CC.3, STOCK.find W1.2, SIGPIPE restore W6.1). I did not expand each — they are mechanical and the finding fixes are correct as written; implementer applies at the cited line.

GATES SUMMARY: Python config/gamelist/guard fixes → verify headless where possible (dry-run + pgrep + 'force a tmp+replace then kill' tests), but the data-loss CLOBBER and all GUI/in-game behavior need on-device. C++ fixes ALL gate on a rebuild (`~/esde-build` → ES-DE-MAD.AppImage) then on-device. The C/CC/N4 leads gate on verification FIRST (they were not adversarially re-checked). The CONTESTED nine gate on a user decision.

batches.shared_approach: All of these ship in ONE rebuild from ~/esde-build (build with the project's existing ES-DE build flow, then install as ~/Applications/ES-DE-MAD.AppImage). None of them touch Python/shell, so the esde_running()/atomic_write()/move_to_tmp() helpers do NOT apply here — those are for the Python batch. The unifying patterns reused from the fork itself: (1) the resetViewVideosTimer() null-guard idiom in ViewController.h:91-95 (`if (mCurrentView != nullptr)`) for the W7.0/W6.0 deref; (2) the std::weak_ptr capture idiom in GuiMadPageBackends.cpp:506-540 (weakChips + `if (auto x = weak.lock())`) for W4.0; (3) the MadScrollView.cpp:79-82 `if (clipDim.x<1||clipDim.y<1) return;` clip guard for CC.4; (4) the MadBackend.cpp:60-68/42-49 sigaction save-in-spawn / restore-in-dtor idiom for W6.1; (5) the rapidjson `IsString()`-then-GetString() guard already used in the surrounding loops for W2.0; (6) std::map::find()-not-at() for W1.2. Every fix is local and additive. Verification is split: a handful (W7.0/W6.0/W4.0/W1.0) need an on-device run because there is no display here — the most we can do headlessly is confirm the build compiles and links (the real gate). I read the git diff base/v3.4.1..HEAD and confirmed all 25 listed files are our patches.
batches.notes:
SEVERITY/SCOPE NOTES the implementer must know before touching anything:

1. W6.0 is a DUPLICATE of W7.0 — same two call sites (main.cpp:555-562 -> ViewController.h:87,89). One fix (the null-guard inside ViewController.h startViewVideos/stopViewVideos/pauseViewVideos/muteViewVideos) resolves BOTH. Do not write two fixes.

2. W4.0 and CC.2 are the SAME bug (CC.2 explicitly says it 'confirms W4.0'). CC.2 is a not-yet-hardened completeness-critic lead but it only sharpens W4.0, which IS hardened (static-confirmed by an executing skeptic). One weak_ptr fix covers both. The W4.0 mechanism is REAL and I re-verified it by reading the code: cycleOption()->setSplash()->(async ok)->rebuildList() at GuiMadPageSplash.cpp:320 destroys mList (and every SwitchComponent in it) at :92; an in-flight splash.toggle_image response then writes sc->setState() through the freed pointer at :195/:203. This is the single highest-value fix in the batch.

3. CC.0 and W6.1 INTERACT and should be coordinated. CC.0 (add PR_SET_PDEATHSIG in the MadWiiBridge child) makes the existing 'PDEATHSIG is the backup' comments TRUE and is the genuinely useful robustness fix. W6.1 (SIGPIPE left ignored process-globally by MadWiiBridge.cpp:51) is LOW value and partly mooted: MadBackend already sets SIGPIPE=SIG_IGN with proper save/restore (MadBackend.cpp:60-68 + dtor :48-49); a launched emulator getting EPIPE instead of dying on SIGPIPE is the benign direction (the code comment at :48-50 says so). I recommend doing CC.0 (high value, trivial) and treating W6.1 as optional polish — see its fix entry.

4. W6.2 and CC.3 are the SAME issue (CC.3 'corroborates W6.2'): std::stoi at ApplicationUpdater.cpp:425 can still throw std::out_of_range on an all-digit string > INT_MAX even after the non-digit guard at :419. One try/catch (or strtol) fixes both. Reachable only via a hand-edited/corrupted feed; updater runs on a background thread but an uncaught exception there still aborts the process.

5. W3.0, W3.1, CC.1, CC.4, CC.5, W0.2, W0.3 are all LOW/INFO 'acceptable as-is' or 'optional hardening' per the findings, and I CONCUR after reading the code. They are cheap and safe to fold into this one rebuild, but none is load-bearing. If the implementer wants a minimal rebuild, the must-do set is: W7.0/W6.0 (crash), W4.0/CC.2 (use-after-free), W7.1 (input-block contract), W1.0 (input wedge), W2.0 (unguarded GetString crash), W1.2 (throwing at()), W4.1+W5.1 (clamp/divide UB), CC.0 (bridge lifetime). The rest are nice-to-have.

6. THINGS THAT GATE ON-DEVICE (no display here, cannot reproduce): W7.0/W6.0 crash repro (needs no-ROMs/invalid-systems state + open Steam overlay/QAM), W4.0 use-after-free window (ideally an ASAN build), W1.0 input wedge (kill the daemon mid-capture), W7.1 stray-press-dismisses-launch-screen. For all of these the headless gate is 'does it still compile/link and does normal MAD navigation still work on-device' — the fixes are defensive and should not change the happy path.

7. I did NOT find additional unfiled C++ items beyond those already in the findings; CC.6/CC.7/CC.8 are positive confirmations (no defect) and need no change.

fixes.reuses: Existing null-guard pattern in the same file: ViewController.h:91-95 resetViewVideosTimer() and :80-83 updateView()
fixes.risk_note: Pure defensive guard. When a view exists (the normal case) behavior is byte-for-byte identical. The only behavior change is: in the no-view state these calls now no-op instead of dereferencing null. No regression to video playback when a view is present.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: rebuild links and `git diff` shows only these 4 lines changed. On-device (gates_on): boot ES-DE with no ROMs (or temporarily invalid es_systems.xml) so the no-games dialog shows, wait >2s, open the Steam overlay/QAM; before the fix this SIGSEGVs, after the fix ES-DE stays alive. Also confirm normal install (with ROMs) still pauses/resumes preview videos on overlay toggle.

fixes.reuses: std::weak_ptr capture idiom at GuiMadPageBackends.cpp:510 (weakChips) with the explicit 'a raw pointer would dangle' comment; the row still owns switchComp via row.addElement(switchComp,false) at :217
fixes.risk_note: Behavior change only in the race window (toggle a pool image, then cycle MODE/FIT before the toggle response lands): before = heap write through a freed SwitchComponent (UAF/corruption); after = the stale setState is skipped (correct — the widget was rebuilt and re-synced from fresh config by rebuildList anyway). The happy path (no rebuild in flight) is unchanged because weakSc.lock() succeeds. The one subtlety to get right: applyConfig() updates mPool which drives the caption on the NEXT rebuild — keep it unconditional in the ok branch.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: rebuild compiles (SwitchComponent is already managed by shared_ptr here, so weak_ptr is valid). On-device/ASAN (gates_on): open MAD -> Splash -> random_image with <=picker-cap images; toggle a pool checkbox and immediately press left/right on MODE or FIT to trigger a rebuild before the response returns; under an ASAN build this previously flagged heap-use-after-free, after the fix it is clean. Functionally confirm the pool toggle still saves and the checkbox reflects saved state.

fixes.reuses: The existing focus-transition block already present at main.cpp:555-563 (just move the setBlockInput into it); mirrors how pauseViewVideos is already transition-gated
fixes.risk_note: This is the trickiest fix because of edge ordering. Consideration: today, if ES-DE loses focus mid-launch-animation, the per-frame write keeps input blocked while unfocused — after the fix, input is blocked on the loss transition and stays blocked (good), then unblocked once on regain. The risk is the regain transition unblocking input while ViewController still wants it blocked (e.g. focus flickers during a game launch). Mitigation suggested in the finding: instead of writing the focus desire directly, OR it with the existing block — but Window::setBlockInput is a plain setter with no read-back of 'who wants it blocked', so a clean OR needs a small refactor. SAFER MINIMAL VERSION acceptable for this rebuild: keep the transition-only write (above); it removes the every-frame clobber which is the actual reported defect (stray press dismissing the launch-screen countdown). Do NOT attempt the full block-source-tracking refactor in this batch unless the user wants it — flag for verify-lead-first if behavior on rapid focus flicker during launch matters.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: rebuild compiles, `git diff` shows the per-frame :549 write removed and the transition branch updated. On-device (gates_on): trigger a game launch (launch-screen countdown visible) and mash a face button — before the fix a stray press can dismiss the launch screen early; after, the launch-animation input-block survives. Also confirm overlay-up still blocks nav and overlay-down restores it.

fixes.reuses: Existing reset points already in the file (onBackendReady :163, :192) prove the maintainers consider mInputLocked safe to force-clear; the topmost-only update() invariant documented at :458-459
fixes.risk_note: Must NOT clear the lock while a capture/tester modal is legitimately open (that would let the panel steal input from a live capture). The whole correctness hinges on accurately knowing 'is a capture modal currently above me'. If GuiMadPanel does not already track that, adding the bool is the safe route; do NOT infer it from mInputLocked itself (circular). Because update() runs only when the panel is topmost, and the modal is pushed ABOVE the panel, the moment the panel's update() runs again the modal has been popped — so checking a panel-owned 'modal open' bool is the right discriminator. Low everyday impact (only bites on daemon death mid-capture).
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. On-device (gates_on): open MAD, start a Daphne/button capture (input locks), then kill mad-backend.py (pkill -f mad-backend.py) so the 'locked:false' never arrives; before the fix the panel is wedged (no input), after the fix the panel regains input within a frame of the modal closing. Confirm a NORMAL capture still locks input for its full duration (don't break the happy path).

fixes.reuses: The IsString()-before-GetString() guard already used in this exact file's other arrays and in GuiMadPageSplash.cpp:61-62
fixes.risk_note: Defensive; well-formed payloads (the only ones the trusted local daemon sends today) are unaffected — every element is already a string. Only changes behavior on a malformed/garbage element (skip instead of crash).
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. Functional: open MAD -> Gamepads with a Wii accessory (nunchuk/classic) and confirm the accessory art/allowed-sprites still render correctly (the guard is a no-op for valid data).

fixes.change:
Replace the throwing `return STOCK.at(key);` with a non-throwing lookup so a future MadColor enum value without a STOCK entry can't throw std::out_of_range out of color(). Change line 203 to:
    const auto stockIt = STOCK.find(key);
    return stockIt != STOCK.cend() ? stockIt->second : 0xFF00FFFF; // magenta = 'missing color' tell
This matches the find()-not-at() style already used throughout color() itself (lines 188-202 all use find()/cend()).

fixes.risk_note: No behavior change for any current MadColor (all 11 are in STOCK). Only changes the impossible-today path (new enum value added without STOCK entry) from a throw/abort to a visible magenta fallback. The findings note 'no such call path exists today' — this is pure future-proofing.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. No runtime change observable today; visual confirmation that MAD panel colors are unchanged on-device.

fixes.reuses: Standard std::swap guard recommended in the finding; pairs with the W5.1 MadStepper guard below
fixes.risk_note: Only triggers on a malformed daemon-supplied min>max (never happens with the current trusted model2 schema). For valid data the swap is a no-op. Removes a UB edge.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. Functional: open MAD -> Model 2, exercise the number steppers (resolution/etc.) and confirm clamping behaves (values stay in range). Valid data path unchanged.

fixes.reuses: Same clamp-the-divisor hardening the finding W5.1 recommends; consistent with W4.1's defensive bounds handling
fixes.risk_note: Every real caller passes a positive step (1.0 or 0.1, see GuiMadPageModel2.cpp:171 and the int stepper at GuiMadPageBackends.cpp:564 passing 1.0f), so this never changes observed behavior; it only removes the divide-by-zero UB if a future caller (or malformed step) passes 0.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. Functional: steppers still increment/decrement by their configured step on-device.

fixes.reuses: The finding's own recommended try/catch; LOG(LogWarning)+continue pattern already used at :421-423 for the non-digit case
fixes.risk_note: Reachable only via a hand-edited/corrupted latest_release.json with an all-digit release number > 2^31-1 (CI run numbers never get there). Updater runs on a background thread but an uncaught std::out_of_range there still terminates the process at startup. Fix changes only the malformed-feed path (skip instead of abort).
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. Hard to exercise without a crafted feed; optionally unit-confirm by pointing mUrl at a local file with releaseNum="99999999999999" and verifying ES-DE logs the warning and starts normally instead of aborting.

fixes.reuses: The 'fail toward focused' safe-default already used at GamescopeFocus.cpp:145-148 and :172 (mMyAppId==0 -> true)
fixes.risk_note: setjmp/longjmp across the Xlib call is the standard (and only correct) way to survive an XIOError without exit(); but longjmp out of Xlib internals can leak whatever Xlib allocated for the in-flight request — acceptable because we are abandoning the connection entirely (mEnabled=false, never poll again this session). Confirm the longjmp target (setjmp) is established on the SAME thread that calls hasFocus() (the render/UI thread — it is, GamescopeFocus is only touched from main.cpp's loop). Do NOT register the handler before mDisplay is set (a failed tryDisplay during init must still just return). Low real-world frequency (gamescope rarely restarts under ES-DE) but the payoff is 'ES-DE survives a gamescope hiccup' vs 'ES-DE exits'.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles and links (X11 already linked per CC.6). On-device (gates_on): hard to force a gamescope X-server drop safely; at minimum confirm normal focus/overlay behavior is unchanged. If feasible, restart gamescope/Steam session while ES-DE runs and confirm ES-DE survives (falls back to always-focused) instead of exiting.

fixes.reuses: The header/inline comments already document PDEATHSIG as the intended mechanism — this makes the code match the documented design
fixes.risk_note: PDEATHSIG fires when the PARENT THREAD that forked dies, not the whole parent process — so if MadWiiBridge::spawn() is called from a thread that later exits while ES-DE lives, the bridge could get a premature SIGTERM. Mitigation: spawn() is called from the main/UI thread at ES-DE startup (single-threaded init), so the forking thread == the process main thread and lives as long as ES-DE. Confirm spawn() is not called from a worker thread. Otherwise harmless: worst case the bridge restarts.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. On-device (gates_on): with a DolphinBar in mode 4, start ES-DE (bridge spawns), then `kill -9` the ES-DE process (bypassing the clean EOF path) and confirm `pgrep -f wii-nav-bridge.py` shows the child has also exited (before the fix, with EOF bypassed, it could linger).

fixes.change:
OPTIONAL / LOW PRIORITY — recommend SKIP or do minimally. MadWiiBridge.cpp:51 sets `signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN)` process-globally and never restores it. Two honest options:
  (a) SKIP IT: MadBackend already saves+restores the real SIGPIPE disposition (MadBackend.cpp:60-68 save in spawn, :48-49 restore in dtor), and the code comment at :48-50 explicitly argues that an emulator receiving EPIPE instead of dying on SIGPIPE is the benign direction. The process-global ignore being left set is low-impact. Given MadWiiBridge is an all-static class with NO destructor (MadWiiBridge.h:17-31), restoring is awkward.
  (b) If you want symmetry: switch the `signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN)` to `sigaction(SIGPIPE, &ignore, &sPrevSigpipe)` saved into a new `static struct sigaction sPrevSigpipe; static bool sSigpipeSaved;`, and restore it in MadWiiBridge::shutdown() (the existing teardown entry point at :119) — mirroring MadBackend's save/restore. This is the only place with a natural restore hook since there's no dtor.
DO NOT regress: do not remove the SIG_IGN entirely — writes to a dead bridge pipe MUST NOT kill ES-DE (the whole point of :48-51).
fixes.reuses: MadBackend.cpp:60-68 (save with sigaction) + dtor restore :48-49 — but adapted to MadWiiBridge::shutdown() since there is no dtor
fixes.risk_note: Honest assessment: this is the lowest-value item in the batch and largely mooted by MadBackend's existing handling. The risk of option (b) is restoring SIGPIPE too early (in shutdown()) while the bridge pipe is still being written elsewhere — but writeLine() guards on sStdinFd>=0 and shutdown() closes it first, so the order is safe. Recommend (a) SKIP unless the user specifically wants the global-state cleanliness.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles (if option b taken). No observable runtime change either way; the bridge already behaves correctly.

fixes.reuses: The EOF/break path already at MadBackend.cpp:141-142 + the mDead/mReaderDone teardown at :162-163; LOG(LogWarning) convention used at :154
fixes.risk_note: Only triggers on a malformed/runaway daemon line that never terminates — which is also a sign the daemon is broken, so treating the stream as corrupt (drop reader -> restart path) is the right response. No legitimate payload approaches 8MB. Confirm breaking here leads poll() to the restart path the same way EOF does (it does: same flags set).
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. Hard to exercise without a misbehaving daemon; optionally inject a giant no-newline write from a stub mad-backend.py and confirm ES-DE logs the warning and restarts the backend instead of growing memory.

fixes.reuses: The existing writeLine()->false->mDead/EBACKEND_DIED path at MadBackend.cpp:405-411; fcntl flag-setting pattern already used in MadWiiBridge.cpp:89 (FD_CLOEXEC)
fixes.risk_note: Behavior change: today a full pipe blocks (UI freezes but the request eventually goes through when the daemon drains); after the fix a full pipe is treated as backend-death (request fails fast, backend restart path). That is arguably MORE correct for a control panel but it DOES change semantics — a transient slow-drain that today recovers would now trigger a restart. Given the daemon is single-client and drains every frame, a genuinely full 64KB pipe means the daemon is wedged, so restart is the right call. Still, flag this for the user: it trades 'rare UI stall' for 'rare spurious restart'. If the user prefers zero behavior change, SKIP W0.1 (it's LOW).
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. On-device (gates_on): normal MAD usage must be unaffected (requests still send). To exercise the new path, stub a daemon that stops reading stdin and confirm ES-DE reports backend-died/restarts instead of freezing.

fixes.reuses: RapidJSON's built-in kParseIterativeFlag; no new code
fixes.risk_note: The iterative parser is a drop-in for the recursive one (same acceptance, slightly different perf characteristics, negligible for KB-sized lines). No behavior change for valid input. Pure stack-overflow defense against a malformed/hostile payload that today can't occur (local trusted daemon).
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. No observable change for normal payloads; confirm MAD pages still parse daemon responses correctly on-device.

fixes.reuses: The joinable()/join() guard already used in shutdownChild() at MadBackend.cpp:479-485
fixes.risk_note: No behavior change on any current path (the thread is always joined before spawn). Purely guards a latent invariant against future refactors. Could be skipped without functional impact.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. No runtime change; normal backend spawn/restart unaffected.

fixes.reuses: Exact sibling guard at MadScrollView.cpp:79-82 (the finding explicitly says to match it)
fixes.risk_note: Cosmetic-only and not currently reachable (no zero-height tile grid arises today). Matches the sibling widget for consistency. For non-degenerate sizes (every real case) behavior is identical.
fixes.verify: Headless gate: compiles. Visual on-device: tile grids (e.g. theme/art pickers) render and clip identically to before.

fixes.change: INFO / DOCUMENT-ONLY (no current bug). The modal manually renders mPanel then itself because Window only renders bottom+top GUI; this is correct ONLY while the modal is the immediate top GUI and the panel is immediately beneath. There is an existing comment at :244-247 explaining the trick — extend it to state the INVARIANT explicitly so a future change that pushes any GUI above an open capture modal doesn't silently break the backdrop. Add to the comment: '// INVARIANT: this assumes the capture modal is the topmost GUI and the panel is the GUI directly beneath it on the Window stack. Do not push another GUI above an open capture modal, or the backdrop will be wrong.' No code change required. (A heavier alternative — render fully opaque so it doesn't depend on mPanel — is not worth it now.)

fixes.risk_note: Comment-only; zero runtime effect. Purely guards future maintainers. Safe to include or skip.

fixes.change: INFO / OPTIONAL — recommend SKIP. debugLog opens+appends+closes an ofstream on every focus transition (cheap, only fires on transitions not per-frame) and the file grows unbounded over a long session. Negligible impact. If desired, the only worthwhile tweak is a size cap: before writing, if the file exceeds e.g. 1MB, truncate/rotate it. Given it's a low-volume diagnostic that the header (:34-36) says is 'safe to leave on', the honest recommendation is to leave it as-is. Do NOT keep the stream open across calls (that complicates lifetime for no real gain on a transition-only path).
fixes.reuses: n/a (recommend no change)

fixes.change: INFO / NO CHANGE NEEDED (self-heals). The file-scope statics sScope/sGamedir/sBase (:22-24) persist across page entries within a session; on panel reopen a stale per-game scope could briefly show a wrong/failed map — but load()'s response handler ALREADY falls back to global on failure (:60-66: if scope=='game' and !ok, reset to global and reload). So it self-corrects within one request. If belt-and-suspenders is wanted, reset the statics to global in the GuiMadPageDaphne destructor, but it's unnecessary. Recommend SKIP.

fixes.risk_note: Already handled by the existing fallback. Any change here is pure cosmetic robustness. Skip unless the user reports a visible wrong-map flash on reopen.
fixes.verify: On-device only if pursued: reopen MAD->Daphne after a per-game scope and confirm no wrong-map flash (already true today).

fixes.change: INFO / ACCEPTABLE AS-IS. mSuppressChildPopRefresh is set true before the apply request (:675) so the child-pop doesn't double-refresh; the apply response refreshes from post-apply truth (:702 refresh()). The theoretical gap (a never-arriving apply response leaves the suppress flag set and skips the pop refresh) is bounded — the next user action or page rebuild refreshes anyway. If full robustness is wanted: in onChildPopped refresh unconditionally and let the apply response's refresh() be idempotent (drop the suppress flag). Recommend SKIP for this rebuild unless the user sees a stale Backends list after an apply.
fixes.reuses: n/a (recommend no change); the existing refresh() at :702 is the truth path
fixes.risk_note: Acceptable per the finding. A dropped apply response is already rare (and itself signals backend trouble that triggers other recovery). Skipping is the honest call.
fixes.verify: On-device only if pursued: apply a profile slot on Backends and confirm the list reflects it (already works).

batches.shared_approach:
All fixes lean on three NEW shared helpers that a separate spec will create in a new module `lib/fsops.py` (chosen because every lib/*_cfg.py and lib/mad_backup.py already do relative imports `from . import X`, and root scripts already use the `sys.path.insert(0, parent); from lib import sgdb` idiom — so both layers can import fsops with zero new plumbing). The three helpers, modeled on patterns ALREADY in the tree:

1) `esde_running() -> bool` — wraps `subprocess.run(["pgrep","-f","ES-DE|emulationstation"], capture_output=True).returncode == 0`, the EXACT guard at steam-collection-sync.py:83. Returns True if ES-DE is up. (A sibling `proc_running(pattern)` generalizes it for the emulator-restore guard, mirroring systems_cmds.py:53 `_retroarch_running` which uses `pgrep -x retroarch`.)

2) `atomic_write(path, data)` — tmp-in-same-dir + os.replace, modeled on retroarch_cfg.py:213 `_atomic_write` and localpolicy.py:89-92. MUST accept BOTH str and bytes (str→write_text(encoding=utf-8), bytes→write_bytes) so it serves XML/cfg writers AND the image-copy path. Does `target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)`, writes `target.with_suffix(target.suffix+'.router-tmp')`, `os.replace(tmp,target)`, and on OSError unlinks the tmp and re-raises (the localpolicy.py:93-99 cleanup contract). For the rpcs3 YAML case, callers serialize to a string first (`yaml.safe_dump(data, sort_keys=...)` to a str) then pass that string.

3) `move_to_tmp(path, task) -> Path` — moves a file into a timestamped recoverable dir on the SAME filesystem and drops a RECOVERY.txt, modeled on steam-collection-sync.py:141-156. For SD-card data (under /run/media/deck/1tbDeck) it uses `TMP_BASE=/run/media/deck/1tbDeck/_TMP_<task>-<ts>` (instant same-fs move per CLAUDE.md rule #5); for /home data it uses `~/Downloads/_TMP-<task>-<ts>`. It returns the dir so callers can log/report the actual path (rule #5 requires reporting it). It should pick the base by checking which filesystem `path` lives on (e.g. compare `path.resolve()` against the /run/media/deck/1tbDeck prefix) so the move is never a slow cross-device copy.

REUSE NOTE: do NOT invent new atomic idioms per-site — every cfg writer below should call fsops.atomic_write so they all share one tested implementation. The gamelist guards are pure additions (a 3-line abort at the top of main()), behavior-preserving when ES-DE is closed (the normal/documented case), so they are the lowest-risk fixes in the batch.
batches.notes:
SCOPE & HONESTY NOTES (the user is non-technical — read these):

• NO C++ in this batch. W8.0/W8.1 are tagged (C) in the scorecard but are actually launchers-tree DATA files (a JSON and a missing JSON under data/gp-defaults/), not ES-DE C++ — so NOTHING here gates on a rebuild. The whole batch ships by editing Python/data files in /home/deck/Emulation/tools/launchers.

• SEVERITY DRIFT — be honest with the user: wave-2 skeptics DOWNGRADED several of these from the headline severity. 10.6→low (a timestamped .bak of the original is already written at steam-fetch-metadata.py:217, so the original is recoverable; atomic write is still worth doing but it is low, not the 'high' the title implies). 9.5/11.4/12.3 are the SAME root bug (the fix_cover 'removed' branch) seen by three finders — implement ONCE, don't write three separate fixes. 6.1 (reset_local unlink) was downgraded to low because it is the user's EXPLICITLY requested 'Reset overrides' action, not accidental clobber — a one-line `.bak` copy before unlink is proportionate; a full _TMP+RECOVERY is overkill but harmless. 11.3 (steam-fetch-media place) downgraded to low (regenerable fetch cache, src and dst are different trees so the copy can't self-destruct) — fix is cheap (copy-to-.part then replace) so still worth it.

• 11.5 (restore-guard) — IMPORTANT CORRECTION to the headline: the wave-2 skeptic verified that backup_targets() (mad_config.py:181) returns ONLY emulator INPUT configs + the local policy file, NOT gamelist.xml/es_settings.xml. So the right guard is the EMULATOR-running / RetroArch-running check (mirror systems_cmds.py:53 `_retroarch_running` + dolphin-wii-mode.sh:32), NOT the ES-DE gamelist guard. Using esde_running() alone would be wrong here. Also a SECOND real defect in the same finding: shutil.copytree(dirs_exist_ok=True) at mad_backup.py:115 MERGES rather than replaces, so a restore is not a faithful revert — that is a separate behavior question; I flag it but recommend leaving the merge semantics alone for now (changing to move-live-dir-to-_TMP-then-copy is a bigger change with its own risk) unless the user wants a true clean revert.

• W8.0 (orphaned romhack-art-urls.json) and W8.1 (missing dualshock4 positions json) are NOT data-safety bugs — they are config-gaps that got swept into this batch by id. W8.0 is dead data (no consumer); the honest fix is a decision, not code (add a pointer comment, OR write the small fetcher mirroring openbor-fetch-media.py, OR move_to_tmp it). fixes.change: place() unlinks existing art BEFORE copying the new file — if the copy fails the old art is gone. Make it copy-then-replace (mirror the safer cdn_download/sgdb_download in the same file which read bytes first). Replace lines 93-97: `for other in (MEDIA/sub).glob(...): other.unlink(); dst = ...; shutil.copy2(src_path, dst)` with: copy to a temp sibling first, then atomically place, then remove stale OTHER-suffix siblings: `dst = MEDIA/sub/(stem + Path(src_path).suffix); part = dst.with_suffix(dst.suffix + '.part'); shutil.copy2(src_path, part); part.replace(dst); for other in (MEDIA/sub).glob(glob.escape(stem)+'.*'): \n    if other != dst: other.unlink()`. This guarantees the existing art survives if copy2 raises (src vanished / ENOSPC), and only removes stale differently-suffixed leftovers AFTER the new file is in place. (Could use fsops.atomic_write for the bytes, but copy2 preserves metadata; part.replace() gives the atomicity. Optionally route the stale-sibling removal through fsops.move_to_tmp, but the finding downgraded this to low/regenerable, so a plain unlink of stale siblings is acceptable.)

batches.shared_approach: SteamOS-update recovery + system scripts. Every C0.x/C1.x lead in this batch was VERIFIED against the real code before designing the fix (all confirmed). Unifying principle across the deck-post-update.sh / samba-setup.sh fixes: each privileged step must (a) be invoked with the elevation it actually needs, and (b) self-balance the steamos-readonly state it touches (disable->work->re-enable), because deck-post-update.sh uses `set -uo pipefail` (NO set -e) so steps run independently and a step that leaves readonly in the wrong state silently breaks a LATER step. Verified readonly chain: samba-setup.sh disables-but-never-re-enables (C0.1); sinden-reinstall-deps.sh (step 2) correctly disable+re-enables, so by step 7 readonly is back ON and the bare pacman (C1.0) fails. The canonical reusable pattern already in-tree is install.sh:146-151 (disable 2>/dev/null||true ; pacman-key init/populate ; pacman ; enable 2>/dev/null||true) and sinden-reinstall-deps.sh:20-21/79-80. Reuse those verbatim rather than inventing. For esde_paths/udev fixes, reuse the existing resolution-order / symlink-resolution patterns already in those files. The three assumed shared helpers (esde_running, atomic_write, move_to_tmp) are NOT relevant to this batch — these are root system scripts, not gamelist/config writers, and touch no user data that needs _TMP.
batches.notes: VERIFICATION RESULT: all eight critic leads in this batch were checked against the real files and ALL are CONFIRMED — none refuted. C0.0 and C1.1 are the SAME bug filed twice (samba never restored: no-sudo caller + EUID==0 hard-exit) — I merged them into one fix (FIX-C0.0-C1.1) so an implementer does not write two conflicting patches. Honesty caveats the user should know: (1) 6.0 is LATENT today — the stale ~/AppDir (Apr 4) and the real running ~/Applications/ES-DE-MAD.AppDir (Jun 13) currently have BYTE-IDENTICAL es_systems.xml, so the wrong-resolution causes no visible bug RIGHT NOW; it only bites after a future ES-DE version bump changes the bundled system set. The fix is still correct and cheap, but it is preventive, not curing a present symptom. (2) The deck-post-update.sh readonly fixes (C1.0) cannot be verified headlessly without actually running pacman on the immutable root, which I did NOT do (no authorization to mutate system state, and CLAUDE.md rule 6 requires explicit authorization for pacman/system changes). They are statically confirmed and the fix mirrors the proven install.sh pattern, but the only true proof is the on-device step after a real SteamOS update. gates_on=verify-lead-first is set where the lead drives a system-root change. (3) NO C++ rebuild needed — this entire batch is shell/Python launcher scripts. (4) Recommended apply ORDER: fix samba-setup.sh (C0.1 re-enable) BEFORE wiring the sudo caller (C0.0/C1.1), otherwise the newly-working samba step would leave the root writable. Each deck-post-update.sh step is independent so order among the post-update fixes themselves does not matter, but C0.1 must land with or before C0.0/C1.1.

FIX: change line 129 from `if [ -x "$T/samba-setup.sh" ]; then bash "$T/samba-setup.sh" || log "  samba-setup.sh returned nonzero"` to call it with elevation: `sudo bash "$T/samba-setup.sh" || log "  samba-setup.sh returned nonzero"`. Do NOT refactor samba-setup.sh to self-elevate — the simpler, lower-risk change is the caller. NOTE: apply FIX-C0.1 (re-enable readonly inside samba-setup.sh) with or before this, or the now-working samba step leaves the immutable root writable.

fixes.change: CONFIRMED: sinden-mpx-setup.sh:69 resolves the P2 keyboard node via a hardcoded `readlink -e /dev/input/event27` — a kernel event number that is NOT stable across reboot/replug. The udev rules file (verified: master == live /etc copy, so editing the master and reinstalling suffices) creates SYMLINKs only for js* and ENV{ID_INPUT_MOUSE} interfaces (lines 18-23), NOT for the keyboard interface. FIX (two coordinated edits): (1) In the master rules file, in the Player-2 block right after line 23, add a keyboard symlink mirroring the mouse rule: `SUBSYSTEM=="input", KERNEL=="event*", ATTRS{idVendor}=="16c0", ATTRS{idProduct}=="0f39", ENV{ID_INPUT_KEYBOARD}=="1", SYMLINK+="input/sinden-gun-p2-kbd", MODE="0660", GROUP="input"`. (Optionally add the symmetric p1-kbd rule for the 0f38 gun for consistency, though only p2 is consumed.) (2) In sinden-mpx-setup.sh:69 replace `P2_KBD_NODE=$(readlink -e /dev/input/event27 2>/dev/null || true)` with `P2_KBD_NODE=$(readlink -f /dev/input/sinden-gun-p2-kbd 2>/dev/null || true)`, mirroring the mouse resolution at line 24 (`readlink -f /dev/input/sinden-gun-p2-event`). fixes.change: CONFIRMED: inside the wrapper heredoc, the re-extract block does `rm -rf "$APPDIR" "$TMP"` then extracts into $TMP then `mv "$SRC" "$APPDIR"`, writing the stamp only on a successful mv; on failure (disk full on /home, power-loss mid-extract) it falls through to `[ -x "$APPDIR/AppRun" ] || exec "$IMG" "$@"` (line 110) — the FUSE-mount path that the whole wrapper exists to AVOID (it re-introduces the request_wait_answer deadlock for native Steam games launched from ES-DE), and it does so SILENTLY. FIX (two cheap guards, both inside the heredoc so they ship in the written wrapper): (1) Before `rm -rf`, check free space against the AppImage size: insert `NEED=$(stat -c '%s' "$IMG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); AVAIL=$(df -kP "$HOME/Applications" 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR==2{print $4*1024}'); if [ "${AVAIL:-0}" -lt "$((NEED*2))" ]; then echo "ES-DE wrapper: low disk on ~/Applications (need ~$((NEED*2/1048576))MB) — keeping existing AppDir, NOT re-extracting" >&2; exec "$APPDIR/AppRun" "$@" 2>/dev/null || exec "$IMG" "$@"; fi` so it does not wipe a working AppDir it cannot replace. (2) Validate the extracted dir before committing: change the `if mv "$SRC" "$APPDIR"; then echo "$WANT" > "$STAMP"; fi` so the stamp is written only if `[ -x "$APPDIR/AppRun" ] && [ -x "$APPDIR/usr/bin/es-de" ]` after the mv. (3) On the fallback `exec "$IMG"` (line 110), prefix a visible warning: `echo "ES-DE wrapper: extraction failed — falling back to FUSE AppImage (Steam-game-launch deadlock possible); free up disk and relaunch" >&2;` before the exec. NOTE the >&2 messages land in ES-DE's startup stream / journal, the only channel available at wrapper time.
fixes.reuses: no existing free-space helper in the repo (grep confirms none) — uses plain df/stat, matching the rest of the script's shell idiom; reuses the script's own `[ -x "$APPDIR/AppRun" ]` validity probe (already at lines 98 and 110)
fixes.risk_note: All edits are inside the WRAPPER HEREDOC, so they take effect only after rewrite_wrapper runs (deck-post-update.sh --wrapper, or step 6, or install.sh). The disk guard prefers keeping a working AppDir over wiping it — strictly safer. The validity check only gates the STAMP write, so a bad extract won't be cached as good. Edge: `df`/`stat`/`awk` must be present (they are on SteamOS). The NEED*2 headroom is a heuristic (extracted AppDir ~= AppImage size); err on generous. Must rewrite the wrapper for the change to land.
fixes.verify: Headless: after editing, run `bash deck-post-update.sh --wrapper` then `bash -n ~/Applications/ES-DE.AppImage` (syntax check the generated wrapper) and `grep -n 'low disk\|extraction failed' ~/Applications/ES-DE.AppImage` to confirm the guards were written. Simulate low disk in a scratch copy by pointing $HOME/Applications at a tmpfs with little free space and confirm it keeps the AppDir + warns instead of FUSE-falling-back. Full on-device proof needs an actual rebuild/AppImage-change to trigger re-extract.

fixes.change: CONFIRMED: the marker $L/.last-os-build is read at line 32 and written ONLY at line 37 (when check_missing returns nothing). Two edge cases: (1) first-run / fresh Deck — .last-os-build absent, `cat` returns '', `$cur` != '' so the FULL check runs and, if anything is missing, the user sees a 'A SteamOS update reset the system and wiped...' dialog (_body, line 19) that MISDESCRIBES a brand-new setup as an update casualty; (2) after a successful manual `deck-post-update.sh` restore, the marker is NOT written by deck-post-update.sh itself, so the nag persists until a later all-present ES-DE launch happens to write it. Read the marker once into a var, e.g. after line 30 add `prev="$(cat "$MARKER" 2>/dev/null)"`, change line 32 to `[ "$cur" = "$prev" ] && exit 0`, and when `missing` is non-empty (line 40-42) branch the dialog title/body on whether `prev` is empty: if `[ -z "$prev" ]` use a first-run message (e.g. title 'Finish MAD setup — run the installer', pointing at install.sh / the first-time setup) vs the existing 'SteamOS updated — run the restore' for the update case. Keep both best-effort (|| true) and never block launch. (B) In deck-post-update.sh, at the very end of a full run (after the '=== done ===' log, line 242), write the marker on success so a completed restore clears the nag immediately: `if check_missing >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf '%s\n' "$(grep -m1 '^BUILD_ID=' /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"')" > "$L/.last-os-build" 2>/dev/null || true; fi`. batches.shared_approach: Two cross-cutting patterns drive this batch. (1) CONCURRENCY ROOT: the mad-backend daemon is NOT single-threaded. rpc.py:24 runs slow=True methods on a 4-worker ThreadPoolExecutor, AND device_cmds.py:128 _WatchStream runs enumerate/scan on its own Stream thread every 2s. So any module-global mutated by a slow method or a Stream can be touched by >=2 threads at once. The seam conclusion (REVIEW-FINDINGS.md line 17) declared 0.2/10.11 DEAD on a "single stdin thread" premise — that premise is WRONG for the daemon (it overlooked _WatchStream + the slow pool). 0.2/10.11 and 2.1 share this root: guard the shared state with a module-level threading.Lock, mirroring the existing _SDL_LOCK (devices.py:394), _WII_LOCK (device_cmds.py:105), _CAM_LOCK (sinden_cmds.py:44) precedents. For 2.1, the cleaner alternative the seam recommends is to make set_ra_option FAST (drop slow=True) so it serializes on the stdin thread like the other tiny config writers model2.set / profiles.apply_slot (both already fast) — the write is small and _retroarch_running() is a quick pgrep. (2) FAIL-SOFT + REUSE: policy parses, regex .group() calls, and shell pipes that currently abort/crash should degrade gracefully, reusing existing guarded patterns already in the same files (the LOCAL-policy try/except in routing.py:51-56 / quit-combo-watcher.py:73; the os.replace atomic_write in retroarch_cfg.py:213; the esde_running()/pgrep guard at steam-collection-sync.py:83; the PIPESTATUS pattern in hypseus-pin.sh:84; the existing _clear() teardown for GUI quit). Reference the spec helpers atomic_write()/move_to_tmp()/esde_running() where they fit, but most fixes here reuse a same-file local equivalent.
batches.notes: HONESTY / CONTEST FLAGS: (a) The seam conclusion is WRONG about 0.2/10.11 being dead. enumerate_devices() IS reached concurrently in the daemon: _WatchStream.run (device_cmds.py:141) calls _scan->enumerate_devices on its own thread while a slow method (devices.sdl, preview.route, gamepads.list, tester.start) calls it on the 4-worker pool. The GIL makes a hard crash unlikely but the unlocked dict read+populate+prune (devices.py:163/193/216-217) can still mis-cache/evict under concurrency. The lock fix is cheap and correct; apply it. Severity stays LOW (self-healing, GIL-protected) — do not over-escalate. (b) 2.1 has a SECOND bug the title omits: _atomic_write (retroarch_cfg.py:213) uses a FIXED shared temp name target.with_suffix(suffix+'.router-tmp'); two concurrent set_system_option calls on the SAME system can make one tmp.replace raise FileNotFoundError (surfaces as EINTERNAL), not just lost-update. Both the lock fix AND making set_ra_option fast close this; making it fast is simplest and matches the other config writers. (c) CONTESTED 10.0: refuter rates verdict 'refuted/uncertain' — the C++ supervisor MadBackend.cpp shutdownChild() hard-caps teardown at ~2s then SIGKILLs, and the only pool subprocess (daphne.bind/hypseus_capture) self-times-out at 10s and holds no grab. So 10.0 is NOT a hang and NOT a grab-leak. Fix is still cheap and correct (daemon=True is already set on the pool via thread_name_prefix? NO — verify: ThreadPoolExecutor workers are non-daemon, so the atexit _python_exit join can add up to ~2s). Design provided but gate on verifying the lead — low priority. (d) CONTESTED 10.3: real outcome (abort on dialog failure) confirmed, but the DISPLAY-failure path is via returncode==1 (not the except branch as the finding claims) AND on a live Gamescope launch the dialog renders fine, so reachability is narrow. Fix is defensible (fail-open on spawn failure) but verify the live behavior first. (e) CONTESTED 8.3: downgraded med->low by refuter — no current victim file can collide with a Bezel-Project .cfg name. Fix (backup-before-overwrite / move_to_tmp) is correct per house rule but low urgency; design provided. (f) N0.0 sub-claim about 'documented trigger-to-quit is dead' is a DOC NIT not a bug — the trigger-quit was deliberately removed (comment at router-config-gui.py:621-625); only the stale comment at 277-278 is wrong. The load-bearing bug (stray nav from gun aim) is real. (g) 0.0: the code ALREADY uses setsid+trap '' TERM for the KILL backstop (lines 113-116); the bug is only the synchronous quit_cmd at line 120 self-SIGTERMing the watcher. The setsid wrapper for the quit_cmd itself is the right fix. (h) 0.1 and 12.4 and C1.2 are the SAME base-policy-parse-unguarded bug on THREE surfaces (routing.py game-launch, lib/policy.py MAD panel) — fix routing.py:48-50 AND lib/policy.py:21-22 together. NONE of these require an ES-DE C++ rebuild; all are Python/shell. The only on-device gates are GUI/hardware behavior verification (4.0/N0.0/N1.0/N6.0/N3.1).

fixes.change: PRIMARY FIX (preferred, matches the seam recommendation): change the decorator at line 242 from `@method("systems.set_ra_option", slow=True)` to `@method("systems.set_ra_option")` so it runs inline on the single stdin thread like the other tiny config writers (model2.set @ model2_cmds.py:198 and profiles.apply_slot @ backends_cmds.py:199 are already fast). The body is a cheap read-modify-write plus one quick pgrep (_retroarch_running) — no SDL init, no device probe, no long file sweep — so it does not belong on the slow pool. This makes all config writers serialize on one thread, eliminating BOTH the lost-update race AND the FileNotFoundError-on-shared-temp-name race. ALTERNATIVE (if it must stay slow for some latency reason): keep slow=True but add a module-level `_RA_OPT_LOCK = threading.Lock()` in retroarch_cfg.py and wrap the entire body of set_system_option (retroarch_cfg.py:317-350) in `with _RA_OPT_LOCK:` so concurrent read-modify-writes serialize; also fix _atomic_write (retroarch_cfg.py:213) to use a UNIQUE temp name (e.g. tempfile.mkstemp in target.parent, or include os.getpid()+threading.get_ident()) so two writers to different systems never collide on the fixed `.router-tmp` name. Recommend the PRIMARY fix — one-line, simpler, no new lock.

fixes.risk_note: PRIMARY: moving set_ra_option off the slow pool means it now runs on the stdin thread; the write touches all of a system's core cfgs (a few small files) — bounded, fast, no blocking syscall, so it will not stall the next request meaningfully. No behavior change for callers (same response shape). The only observable effect is correct serialization. The ALTERNATIVE lock approach is also low risk but adds a lock + a temp-name change.

fixes.change: Add a module-level lock next to the cache declaration: after line 134 (`_ENUM_CACHE: dict = {}`) add `_ENUM_CACHE_LOCK = threading.Lock()` (threading is already importable — _SDL_LOCK at line 394 uses it; add `import threading` at top if not already present, confirm first). Then hold the lock around the cache reads/writes/prune inside enumerate_devices: simplest safe approach is to wrap the whole per-node loop body's cache access and the final prune. Concretely: take `with _ENUM_CACHE_LOCK:` around the block that does `hit = _ENUM_CACHE.get(path)` (163) through `_ENUM_CACHE[path] = (sig, f)` (193) for each node, and a second `with _ENUM_CACHE_LOCK:` around the prune loop at 216-217. (The evdev open/close at 168-192 is the expensive part and need not be under the lock if you restructure, but the straightforward correct fix is to hold it for the whole function body — the close cost only hits genuinely new nodes, and concurrent enumerations are rare, so contention is negligible.) NOTE: this contradicts the seam conclusion which called this DEAD — it is NOT dead because _WatchStream (device_cmds.py:128) calls _scan->enumerate_devices on its OWN thread while slow methods (devices.sdl, preview.route, gamepads.list, tester.start) call it on the pool. Document that in a one-line comment.
fixes.reuses: _SDL_LOCK pattern in the same file (devices.py:394, used at :422); _WII_LOCK (device_cmds.py:105)
fixes.risk_note: Adds a lock around a function that can be called from multiple daemon threads; holding it across the evdev open/close serializes concurrent full walks (so two simultaneous walks run one-after-another instead of interleaving). Worst case is a brief added latency when _WatchStream and a slow probe coincide — acceptable and far better than cache corruption. The single-shot router process only ever calls it once, so it is unaffected. No API change.
fixes.verify: Headless: `python3 -c "from lib import devices; import threading; [t.start() for t in [threading.Thread(target=devices.enumerate_devices) for _ in range(6)]]"` and confirm no exception and stable cache; `python3 -m py_compile lib/devices.py`. The race is probabilistic so absence of an error is not proof — rely on static reasoning (the with-lock makes the dict ops mutually exclusive).

fixes.change: The watcher self-SIGTERMs because line 120 runs `subprocess.run(quit_cmd, shell=True)` SYNCHRONOUSLY in-process, and quit_cmd's own `pkill -TERM -f 'Eden|Yuzu|...'` matches the watcher's own argv (the pattern is on the watcher's /proc cmdline via the game-start hook). Fix: run the quit_cmd in its own detached session so the watcher is out of the pkill blast radius, mirroring how the KILL backstop already protects itself (lines 113-116 already use `setsid` + `trap '' TERM`). Change line 120 to: `subprocess.Popen(["setsid", "bash", "-c", quit_cmd], stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)` then `log('quit command sent (detached)')` and `return` immediately from main() (the watcher's job is done once the quit is dispatched). Because setsid detaches it into a new session/process group, the quit_cmd's `pkill -TERM` no longer hits the watcher, so the quit_cmd's OWN `sleep 2; pkill -KILL` fast-escalation (controller-policy.toml:411-414) runs as the policy author intended (2s, not the 6s global backstop). Keep the existing global setsid KILL backstop (113-116) as the safety net.
fixes.reuses: the existing setsid + DEVNULL pattern already in _quit at lines 113-116
fixes.risk_note: Changing subprocess.run (blocking) to Popen+setsid (detached, non-blocking) means _quit returns immediately and the watcher exits without waiting for the quit_cmd to finish. That is the intended behavior (the quit_cmd handles its own escalation). RISK: ensure the watcher's `finally` fd-close (199-203) still runs — it will, because main() returns normally. Also confirm setsid is on PATH (it is on SteamOS — already used at line 115). The Hypseus backend's `[h]ypseus.real` bracket trick is unaffected (it never matched the watcher). Edge: if a quit_cmd relies on running in the watcher's session it would change, but these are pkill/flatpak-kill one-liners that do not.

fixes.change: Line 131 `text = _DISPLAY_RE.sub(rf"\g<1>{dev.name}\g<3>", text, count=1)` interpolates the raw evdev device name into a re.sub REPLACEMENT template, where backslashes and `\g<n>` sequences are special — a name with `\`, `&`, `<`, `>` corrupts the controllerN.xml or raises. Fix two things: (1) treat the value LITERALLY via a function replacement so backslashes/group-refs are not interpreted, and (2) XML-escape the name since it lands inside an XML element. Add at top of file (after `import re`, line 32): `from xml.sax.saxutils import escape as _xml_escape`. Then change line 131 to: `text = _DISPLAY_RE.sub(lambda m: m.group(1) + _xml_escape(dev.name) + m.group(3), text, count=1)`. _DISPLAY_RE has 3 capture groups (cemu_cfg.py:39: `(<display_name>)(.*?)(</display_name>)`), so m.group(1)/m.group(3) are the open/close tags. ALSO apply the same lambda+escape pattern to the _UUID_RE.sub at line 130 if new_uuid could ever contain a backslash (it is `f'{sdl_index}_{guid}'` where guid is hex — currently safe, but the lambda form is strictly safer; optional). This is the same bug/fix as sibling finding 5.1 (cemu_cfg.py:130-131) — fix both lines together.
fixes.reuses: xml.sax.saxutils.escape (stdlib); lambda-replacement is the standard fix for re.sub backreference injection (also recommended in finding 5.1)
fixes.risk_note: Behavior change only for device names containing regex/XML-special chars; common pad names (alphanumerics + spaces) produce identical output to today (escape() is a no-op on them, and the lambda emits the same literal). No change to the UUID logic unless you also convert line 130. The XML-escape is a correctness improvement (Cemu reads this as XML) and matches what should have been there.

On-device: launch a Cemu game with a pad renamed to include `&`.

fixes.change: Line 72 `dirn=re.search(r'^DIR=(.*)$', man, re.M).group(1)` calls .group(1) directly on the search result; a manifest with no DIR= line returns None and raises AttributeError, crashing the whole media fetch. Guard it: replace line 72 with `dm = re.search(r'^DIR=(.*)$', man, re.M); dirn = dm.group(1) if dm else ''`. The two following loops (73-76) already test `if dirn and dirn in path` (73) so an empty dirn naturally skips the path-match loop; the name-match loop at 75-76 (`if name==dirn or name==stem`) still works via the `name==stem` fallback. So with dirn='' the function falls through to `return None` (77) for a truly unmatchable game, skipping it instead of crashing the run. This mirrors the same fix already applied at line 68-71 for the PREFIX line (which uses `if m:`).

fixes.change: The script is `#!/bin/sh` and runs `{ ...; supermodel-sinden-smart.py "$@" 2>&1; } 2>&1 | tee "$LOG"` with no pipefail/PIPESTATUS, so the script's exit status = tee's status = 0 always — a failed launch (smart launcher error, or os.execvpe failing to exec supermodel) is invisible to ES-DE. Fix mirroring the sibling hypseus-pin.sh (which uses bash + `exit "${PIPESTATUS[0]}"` at line 84): (1) change the shebang line 1 to `#!/usr/bin/env bash`; (2) after the closing `} 2>&1 | tee "$LOG"` (line 15) add a new line `exit "${PIPESTATUS[0]}"` so the inner command's status (the smart launcher, which os.execvpe-replaces itself with supermodel — so this is supermodel's own exit code) propagates to ES-DE. PIPESTATUS[0] is the first pipeline element (the brace group), whose status is the last command inside it (the .py). bash on SteamOS supports PIPESTATUS (already relied upon by hypseus-pin.sh).
fixes.reuses: the bash-shebang + `exit "${PIPESTATUS[0]}"` pattern from hypseus-pin.sh:1,84
fixes.risk_note: Switching sh->bash is safe on SteamOS (bash is present; the script uses no sh-only constructs). The only behavior change is that ES-DE now sees the real exit code instead of always 0 — i.e. a failed Supermodel launch will now correctly surface as a launch failure. Confirm the game-end hooks/controller-router cleanup are OK with a nonzero exit (they should be; they run regardless). No change to the tee logging.
