fixes.change: The headless X-Arcade escape (line 827 `both = self.spots.get('mouse1') and self.spots.get('mouse2')`) derives the P1+P2-Start gesture from the CALIBRATABLE spot dict (spots['mouse1']/['mouse2'] are only set when BTN_START maps to those spots via self.cal at line 722/673-674). If the user re-calibrates Start to a different spot, the escape breaks or fires on the wrong buttons; and a mid-calibration Start press can end the test, discarding unsaved bindings. Fix mirroring the Tk version (_xa_quit_check, mad_xarcade_tester.py:728-730 which counts raw `f"{od['tag']}:k{e.BTN_START}"` keys in self._xa_pressed): (1) maintain a raw pressed-set keyed by node+BTN_START directly from the event stream. In _event (716-727), in the EV_KEY branch, regardless of calibration, track Start: `if ev.code == e.BTN_START and tag in ('P1','P2'): self._start_held = getattr(self,'_start_held',set()); (self._start_held.add(tag) if ev.value else self._start_held.discard(tag))`. (2) In the run loop replace line 827 with a raw count gated on not-calibrating: `both = (self._cal_armed is None) and len({t for t in getattr(self,'_start_held',set())}) >= 2` (i.e. both P1 and P2 Start physically held AND not mid-calibration). Initialize `self._start_held = set()` in __init__ (near line 655-660). This makes the escape immune to calibration and prevents a mid-calibrate Start+Start from ending the test. Read 716-727 and 826-839 carefully before editing to confirm the EV_KEY branch placement.

fixes.change: Both media-fetch scripts mkdir -p into hardcoded /run/media/deck/1tbDeck/... paths with no mount check; when the SD is unmounted they create the tree on the root tmpfs/partition, silently filling root and shadowing the real card on remount. Fix: add a one-time mount/existence assertion at the START of main() before the first mkdir, aborting with a clear message. For steam-fetch-media.py, in main() (line 183) before the mkdir loop (188) add: `import os.path as _osp\nif not _osp.ismount('/run/media/deck/1tbDeck'):\n    print('SD card /run/media/deck/1tbDeck is not mounted — aborting (refusing to write media to the root partition).'); return`. For openbor-fetch-media.py, add the same guard at the top of main() (line 171) before games()/copy_art run. Use os.path.ismount on the VOLUME ROOT '/run/media/deck/1tbDeck' (not the downloaded_media subdir, which would not be a mountpoint). NOTE: the volume name '1tbDeck' is a documented project constant (CLAUDE.md confirms /run/media/deck/1tbDeck is THIS deck's card and finding 11.8 refuted the 'hardcoded name' concern), so ismount on that literal path is acceptable here; do not over-engineer a MediaDirectory-derivation unless the user asks.
fixes.reuses: os.path.ismount (stdlib); the early-abort+message pattern from steam-collection-sync.py:80-86
fixes.risk_note: Adds a guard that only triggers when the SD is genuinely unmounted (rare on a normally-running Deck where the card is the documented constant). On the happy path (card mounted) ismount returns True and nothing changes. CONTESTED-adjacent: this is a critic LEAD (C1.3), not a hardened finding — the mechanism (root fill on unmount) is real and the guard is cheap and harmless, so it is safe to add, but flag it as a lead. Do not also try to derive the path from ES-DE settings unless requested (over-engineering).
fixes.verify: Headless: `python3 -c "import os.path; print(os.path.ismount('/run/media/deck/1tbDeck'))"` (True now, since the card is mounted); to test the abort, point the guard at a known-unmounted path temporarily and confirm main() returns with the message. `python3 -m py_compile steam-fetch-media.py openbor-fetch-media.py`.

fixes.change: CONTESTED — the refuter rates this refuted/uncertain: the C++ supervisor MadBackend.cpp shutdownChild() hard-caps teardown at ~2s then SIGKILLs, and the only pool subprocess (daphne.bind/hypseus_capture) self-times-out at ~10s and holds no evdev grab — so this is NOT a hang and NOT a grab-leak, only a minor up-to-~2s slower exit when a slow method is in flight at teardown (the ThreadPoolExecutor atexit _python_exit join is non-daemon). Design IF the user wants the polish: in rpc.py add a teardown helper `def shutdown_pool():\n    _POOL.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)` and call it from mad-backend.py's finally block right after stop_all_streams() (line ~159-162). cancel_futures requires Python 3.9+ (Deck has 3.13, fine). This lets the daemon exit promptly instead of waiting on in-flight pool tasks. Do NOT claim it fixes a hang or a subprocess orphan — it does not; the SIGKILL path and the child self-timeout already cover those. Also note the simplest partial mitigation (make pool threads daemon=True) is NOT available via ThreadPoolExecutor's public API, so use shutdown(cancel_futures=True).
fixes.reuses: stop_all_streams teardown call site (mad-backend.py:159-162); ThreadPoolExecutor.shutdown stdlib API
fixes.risk_note: cancel_futures=True drops not-yet-started tasks and wait=False does not block on running ones — so a slow method running at teardown is abandoned (its response never sent), which is fine because the panel is already gone. RISK: if any slow method holds a resource that ONLY its own finally releases (e.g. a temp file), abandoning it could leak that — but grabs/children are in Streams (covered by stop_all_streams) or self-bounded subprocesses, so this is low. Since this is contested and low-value, gate it behind verifying the lead first.
fixes.verify: Headless: reproduce the wave2 measurement — a script submitting a 3s pool task then sys.exit(0) takes ~3s without shutdown and ~0s with shutdown(cancel_futures=True). Confirm mad-backend still exits 0 on EOF. `python3 -m py_compile lib/madsrv/rpc.py mad-backend.py`.

fixes.change: CONTESTED (downgrade med->low per refuter). The warning dialog's _show_warning_blocking returns the child exit code and callers treat ANY non-zero as Cancel -> abort the launch. The refuter showed: (i) a headless/broken DISPLAY does NOT hit the except branch — subprocess.run returns returncode==1 (so the abort comes via the normal return path, not the except), and (ii) on a real Gamescope launch the dialog renders fine, so reachability is narrow. Design IF pursued: distinguish 'dialog failed to display' from 'user pressed Cancel'. Make lib/warning_dialog emit a DISTINCT exit code on a Tk/display failure (e.g. exit 2 for 'could not show dialog') vs exit 1 for an actual user Cancel; then in controller-router.py treat returncode==1 as Cancel (abort) but returncode in (2, other) as PROCEED (return 0, launch un-warned) so a broken warning UI never blocks the game. Also keep the existing env.setdefault('DISPLAY', ':0') at line 101. VERIFY THE LIVE BEHAVIOR FIRST — the refuter notes the reviewer's headless tty is NOT the live ES-DE/Gamescope launch env, so the failure may not occur in practice; do not change fail-closed-to-fail-open without confirming the warning UI genuinely fails on a real Deck launch (a protective warning failing CLOSED is a defensible default).
fixes.reuses: the existing exit-code return contract in _show_warning_blocking; the env.setdefault DISPLAY guard at controller-router.py:101
fixes.risk_note: Changing fail-closed to fail-open for the warning dialog means that if the warning UI is genuinely broken, the game launches WITHOUT the user seeing the (e.g. 'no lightgun connected') warning. That is a deliberate trade-off and could mask a real misconfiguration. Because the path is narrow and contested, and the current fail-closed behavior is defensible, this should only be done after confirming the live failure exists. Requires touching both warning_dialog.py (new exit code) and controller-router.py (caller logic).
fixes.verify: On-device: force a warning condition (e.g. require_sinden with no gun) on a real Gamescope launch and observe whether the dialog shows; if it shows fine, this fix is unnecessary. If it genuinely fails to show and aborts the launch, confirm the new exit-2 path lets the game proceed. Headless: `DISPLAY=:99 python3 -m lib.warning_dialog T B; echo $?` to see the current failure exit code.

fixes.change: CONTESTED (downgraded med->low per refuter — no current victim file can collide with a Bezel-Project .cfg name, since those are ROM-named per-GAME cfgs and the irreplaceable user data is per-CORE/per-CONTENT cfgs which $game never produces). The `cat > "$core_dir/$game.cfg" <<EOF` truncates in place with no backup, violating the house 'never destroy user data' rule. Design IF pursued: before the `cat >`, if the target exists AND is not already a prior bezel/wire-bezels output, move it to a recoverable _TMP (per project rule #5 / the move_to_tmp helper) rather than clobbering. Concretely, before line 74's heredoc add: `target="$core_dir/$game.cfg"; if [ -f "$target" ] && ! grep -q 'bezelproject\|wire-bezels' "$target"; then mkdir -p "$TMP/_bezel-overwrite-$(date +%s)"; mv "$target" "$TMP/_bezel-overwrite-.../"; fi` (use the SD-card _TMP root /run/media/deck/1tbDeck/_TMP_bezel-<ts> for instant same-fs move, and write a RECOVERY.txt). Keep the same change in install-bezels-all.sh:95-103. Apply the same pattern to both scripts. LOW priority given the refuter's no-victim finding; do this for rule compliance, not urgency.
fixes.reuses: the move_to_tmp() spec helper / the project _TMP+RECOVERY.txt convention (CLAUDE.md rule #5)
fixes.risk_note: The guard only moves a file that exists AND lacks a bezel/wire-bezels marker (i.e. a genuine hand-made override), so re-running a bezel install over its own prior output is unaffected (the script's documented idempotency is preserved). RISK: the grep heuristic for 'is this a prior bezel config' must match what the script itself writes — verify the heredoc body contains a recognizable marker comment; if not, add one to the heredoc so future runs can detect their own output. Contested/low-value: design provided, but it is a house-rule compliance fix, not an active-bug fix.
fixes.verify: Headless: seed a fake $core_dir/$game.cfg with hand-tuned content (no bezel marker), run the guarded snippet, confirm the file is MOVED to _TMP (not truncated) and a RECOVERY.txt is written; seed a bezel-marked cfg and confirm it is overwritten in place (idempotent). `bash -n install-bezels.sh install-bezels-all.sh`.

verdicts.concern: Core claim verified: main.cpp:549 does an UNCONDITIONAL `window->setBlockInput(!esHasFocus)` every frame, which clobbers ViewController's intentional blocks (confirmed call sites: ViewController.h:101 triggerGameLaunch, ViewController.cpp:1086/1668/1691/1706 launch/rescan). The transition branch (554-564) the fix wants to fold into ALREADY EXISTS and already manages sHadFocus — so the fix is a small, correct refactor, not a new mechanism. Two residual risks the fix should acknowledge: (1) if a game launch is triggered while UNFOCUSED, the focus-regain `setBlockInput(false)` would unblock prematurely — an edge case, but the launch-then-overlay path exists; (2) lines 589/652 are inside `#if defined(__ANDROID__)` so no conflict on Deck. Behavior change is real (moderate risk) but strictly better than the per-frame clobber.
verdicts.adjustment: Keep the proposed transition-gated setBlockInput, but on focus REGAIN do NOT force `setBlockInput(false)` unconditionally — instead only clear the focus-derived block, e.g. preserve any block ViewController set: gate the unblock on `!ViewController::getInstance()->getGameLaunchTriggered()` (that getter exists at ViewController.h:103) so a launch-in-progress block survives focus regain. Verify on-device: trigger a game launch then open/close the Steam overlay during the launch countdown and confirm the launch screen is not dismissible.

verdicts.concern: The fix's central premise is WRONG. It claims 'GuiMadPanel already pushes GuiMadCaptureModal (it owns the push site)' and that captureModalActive() is easy to implement by tracking the panel's own push. It does NOT: GuiMadCaptureModal is pushed by the PAGES (GuiMadPagePlayers.cpp:128, GuiMadPagePreview.cpp:278, GuiMadPageQuitCombo.cpp:322/724/858) via mWindow->pushGui — never by GuiMadPanel. So a `mCaptureModalOpen` bool in the panel would have to be wired through five page call sites. Worse, the fix's other premise — 'GuiMadPanel::update() only runs when the panel is topmost, so if update() runs the modal can't be up' — actually UNDERMINES the need for the check: while a capture modal is live it is topmost and renders the panel itself (GuiMadCaptureModal::render at :248-250), so GuiMadPanel::update() does NOT run during a capture. Thus by the time update() runs again the modal is already popped, and onBackendReady() already force-clears mInputLocked on a daemon restart (:163). The genuine residual gap (daemon died, modal popped, daemon not yet restarted) is narrow and the proposed reset could mis-fire. Risk: clearing mInputLocked while a legitimate lock is briefly held could let input leak to the views.
verdicts.adjustment: Do NOT implement captureModalActive() via a panel-owned bool. First VERIFY the residual gap is real on-device (kill the daemon mid-capture, confirm input wedges with the modal already gone). If real, the robust fix is daemon-side or via the modal's own lifetime: have GuiMadCaptureModal clear the panel's lock in its destructor/close path (the modal already holds mPanel), OR add a wall-clock watchdog in GuiMadPanel::update() that clears mInputLocked + mInputLockAllowNav if it has been set for longer than the longest legitimate capture (e.g. >30s) with no input.lock refresh. Treat W1.0 as needs-verification-first, not a ready fix.

verdicts.concern: Verified. GuiMadPageGamepads.cpp:490-492 calls allowedArr[i].GetString() with no IsString() guard; rapidjson asserts/UB on a non-string element. Verified. MadTheme.cpp:203 `return STOCK.at(key)` throws std::out_of_range if a MadColor enum value has no STOCK entry. Every other lookup in color() (:188-202) uses find()/cend(). Verified. GuiMadPageModel2.cpp:172 std::clamp(value, lo, hi) is UB if hi < lo, and lo/hi come from daemon-supplied min/max (numberAt at :169-170) with no ordering guarantee. The `if (hi < lo) std::swap(lo, hi)` guard before clamp is correct and also protects the downstream MadStepper (constructed with lo,hi at :174-175). The fix correctly notes lo/hi must drop const (they are currently `const float`). <algorithm> is needed for std::clamp (already used) and <utility> for std::swap.

Clamping mStep to a tiny positive floor (1e-6f) in the init list prevents div-by-zero. verdicts.concern: Verified. ApplicationUpdater.cpp:425 `std::stoi(releaseType->releaseNum)` still throws std::out_of_range for an all-digit string > INT_MAX, defeating the :419-424 non-digit guard whose stated goal is 'must not throw on the updater thread'. The try/catch + LOG(LogWarning) + continue fix is sound and `continue` is correct (inside the release loop). CC.3 note is correct: the Android std::stoi at :409 is under `#if defined(__ANDROID__)` (confirmed at :404) so it does not compile in the Deck build — leaving it is right.

Verified the mechanism: GamescopeFocus holds a long-lived Display* (mDisplay set at :108) polled every frame in hasFocus() (:139-154). Xlib's default IO-error handler calls exit() when the connection drops (gamescope restart), terminating ES-DE. The XSetIOErrorHandler + longjmp approach is the correct, standard idiom (an XIOErrorHandler MUST NOT return normally or Xlib exits anyway — the fix correctly notes this). 'Fail toward focused' matches the existing safe default (:145-148, :172). Two cautions: (a) setjmp/longjmp across the readCardinal calls is fragile re: local non-trivial C++ objects with destructors in scope between setjmp and longjmp — the polling block uses only PODs (unsigned longs), so it is acceptable here, but the setjmp must be placed so no non-trivial locals are skipped; (b) longjmp out of an Xlib internal callback is generally tolerated but technically Xlib may hold internal locks — on a dying connection this is the lesser evil vs exit(). MODERATE risk, hard to verify headless.
verdicts.adjustment: Place setjmp at the very top of the polling block (before any non-POD local is constructed) — the current locals (focusedApp etc. at :140-143) are PODs so this is fine. Mark mDisplay=nullptr and mEnabled=false in the longjmp-return branch (as the fix does) so subsequent frames take the early `if (!mEnabled) return true` at :127 and never touch the dead Display*. Verify on-device by restarting gamescope/Steam while ES-DE is open and confirming ES-DE survives (no display here to test).

verdicts.concern: Verified and the fix is MORE justified than it claims. wii-nav-bridge.py:419 DOES already set PR_SET_PDEATHSIG (ctypes prctl(1, SIGTERM)) — so the C++ add is NOT purely the-real-fix, but it is NOT merely redundant either: the Python prctl runs only after main() reaches :419 (after flock acquisition at :408-415), leaving a genuine startup-race window where the parent could die before :419 executes, orphaning the child with no PDEATHSIG. Setting prctl in the C++ child immediately after fork closes that window. So this is a real (if narrow) improvement, harmless belt-and-suspenders with the Python side. The placement (before execlp at :80) is correct.
verdicts.adjustment: Note in the implementation that this complements (does not replace) the Python-side prctl at wii-nav-bridge.py:419 — it specifically closes the fork→reach-line-419 startup race. PR_SET_PDEATHSIG survives execve (it is only cleared on a UID change, which does not occur here), so setting it before execlp is effective. Keep `#include <sys/prctl.h>`.

verdicts.concern: Verified. MadWiiBridge.cpp:51 uses the legacy `signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN)` process-globally and never restores it; MadBackend.cpp:60-68 by contrast saves the previous disposition with sigaction(&mPrevSigpipe) and restores it. The fix's recommendation to SKIP (option a) is the correct call: MadWiiBridge is all-static with no dtor, MadBackend already manages the real SIGPIPE save/restore, and leaving SIG_IGN set is benign (the codebase's own comment at MadBackend :48-50 argues EPIPE-instead-of-death is the safe direction). The fix correctly warns NOT to remove SIG_IGN. Low value, low risk either way.
verdicts.adjustment: Recommend SKIP (option a). If symmetry is wanted, option b (sigaction save into static + restore in MadWiiBridge::shutdown()) is acceptable but adds state for negligible benefit. Do not block the rebuild on this.

verdicts.concern: Verified. readerLoop (MadBackend.cpp:130-164) appends to `buffer` (:143) and only drains complete newline-terminated lines; a daemon line with no newline grows buffer unbounded. The fix's cap-and-break correctly mirrors the EOF path: `break` falls through to mDead=true/mReaderDone=true at :162-163, which poll()'s death/restart handling already covers. 8MB is generously above any legitimate NDJSON line. Placement after the inner drain loop / buffer.erase at :159 is correct.

The proposed non-blocking fix would CORRUPT the wire protocol. writeLine() (MadBackend.cpp:414-430) is a partial-write loop: `while (written < payload.length())`. If mStdinFd is set O_NONBLOCK and a line partially writes then the pipe fills, write() returns -1/EAGAIN MID-LINE — the proposed handler returns false ('backend died'), but a PARTIAL NDJSON line has ALREADY been sent to the daemon, desyncing the protocol, AND the backend is falsely declared dead when it was merely briefly full. The pipes are created with pipe2(O_CLOEXEC) at :79 (not O_NONBLOCK), confirming the code currently relies on blocking semantics for atomicity of small writes. The finding itself is rated LOW/speculative (the daemon drains continuously, lines are tiny KB NDJSON well under the 64KB pipe buffer, so a single writeLine never blocks in practice).
verdicts.adjustment: Recommend SKIP / defer. The blocking-UI-thread risk is largely theoretical given tiny requests vs a 64KB pipe and a continuously-draining daemon. If pursued, do NOT make the write non-blocking with the current partial-write loop. A safe alternative: keep blocking writes but bound them with a poll(POLLOUT) timeout before each write() and treat a timeout as backend-died — that fails a request cleanly without sending a partial line. Treat as needs-design, not a ready fix.

verdicts.concern: Verified. MadJson::parseLine (MadJson.h:25-29) calls `doc.Parse(line.c_str(), line.length())` with no flags; rapidjson/document.h is included at :12. Switching to `doc.Parse<rapidjson::kParseIterativeFlag>(...)` bounds stack usage against deeply-nested input. Purely defensive (the trusted local daemon never sends deep nesting). Trivial, no behavior change for normal input.

verdicts.concern: Verified. The inline check at steam-collection-sync.py:82-83 is exactly `subprocess.run(['pgrep','-f','ES-DE|emulationstation'], capture_output=True).returncode == 0`, and the regex must stay 'ES-DE|emulationstation' (matches both the AppImage 'ES-DE' process and legacy 'emulationstation'). lib/__init__.py exists so a new lib/proc_guard.py imports cleanly. verdicts.concern: Verified the three source copies match: retroarch_cfg._atomic_write (lib/retroarch_cfg.py:213 — uses '.router-tmp', does parent mkdir + tmp.replace), localpolicy.dump (lib/localpolicy.py — '.tmp', os.replace, unlink-on-OSError-and-reraise), sinden_cfg (line ~127 — '.tmp', os.replace). The canonical helper correctly folds these. TWO guards the spec is silent on: (1) PERMISSIONS/OWNERSHIP — os.replace(tmp, target) replaces the target inode, so the new file gets the tmp's perms (umask-derived), NOT the original target's. For gamelists/emulator configs this is usually fine, but for the GLOBAL retroarch.cfg (finding 8.2) it could silently reset perms. Add shutil.copystat(target, tmp) before replace when target exists. (2) backup_once_suffix is NOT one-size-fits-all: cemu_cfg already uses a DIRECTORY-based backup (_BACKUP_DIRNAME='.router-backup', lib/cemu_cfg.py:40/61) backing up multiple per-port files — routing cemu through the helper's single-file backup_once_suffix would create a DIFFERENT/duplicate backup scheme. pcsx2 (.router-backup sibling file) and sinden/supermodel map cleanly.
verdicts.adjustment: Add `if target.exists(): shutil.copystat(target, tmp)` before os.replace (preserve mode), and ensure target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) is unconditional (it is, in the spec). Do NOT route cemu_cfg (5.0/10.7) through backup_once_suffix — keep cemu's existing dir-based _backup_once and only adopt the ATOMIC-WRITE part of the helper for it. Use backup_once_suffix only for pcsx2/xemu/eden/rpcs3/sinden/supermodel single-file targets.

verdicts.concern: Verified. tester_cmds._write_json (lib/madsrv/tester_cmds.py:228-230) currently does parent mkdir + non-atomic path.write_text(json.dumps(...)). Routing it (and the Tk testers' JSON saves) through atomic_write_json (which delegates to atomic_write_text) is sound and consistent. No behavior change beyond atomicity. Inherits the same perms caveat as atomic_write_text (negligible for these JSON state files in user dirs).
verdicts.adjustment: Change the last line of tester_cmds._write_json to call atomic_write_json (keep its existing parent.mkdir or rely on atomic_write_text's). Confirm json.dumps(indent=2) matches the current format so diffs/round-trips are stable.

verdicts.concern: Verified. A generalized `pgrep -f <pattern>` wrapper is sound; esde_running() correctly becomes process_running('ES-DE|emulationstation'). For finding 11.5 (mad_backup.do_restore), refusing a restore while cemu/pcsx2/rpcs3/xemu/eden/ES-DE is open is the right guard. do_restore (lib/mad_backup.py:102) is called from the interactive MAD GUI, so a hard-stop + message is appropriate (not blocking a legitimate non-interactive use). NOTE: `pgrep -f` matches the full command line, so an emulator pattern like 'rpcs3' could match an unrelated process with that string in argv (e.g. a log path) — low risk but use specific patterns.

Place the guard at the start of do_restore (or its GUI call site) returning a clear 'close <emulator> first' message rather than proceeding with a partial restore.

critic.overall_note:
I opened the actual code for every C++ finding and verified the key shared-helper source patterns. Most fixes are sound and faithfully reuse existing in-repo patterns. Two are NOT sound as written and must change:

(1) W1.0 — the fix's premise is factually wrong: GuiMadCaptureModal is pushed by the PAGES (GuiMadPagePlayers/Preview/QuitCombo), NOT by GuiMadPanel, so a panel-owned captureModalActive() bool cannot be implemented as described without wiring through 5 page call sites. Moreover, because the modal is topmost and renders the panel itself during a capture, GuiMadPanel::update() does NOT run during a capture — so the 'if update() runs, no modal can be up' logic largely obviates the proposed check, and onBackendReady() (:163) already force-clears the lock on daemon restart. This should be marked verify-first and, if real, fixed via the modal's own lifetime (clear the lock in the modal's destructor/close) or a wall-clock watchdog, not a panel bool.

(2) W0.1 — making mStdinFd O_NONBLOCK with the current partial-write loop in writeLine() would send a partial NDJSON line then return 'backend died' on EAGAIN, corrupting the wire protocol and falsely declaring death. The finding is LOW/speculative anyway (tiny requests, 64KB pipe, continuously-draining daemon). Recommend skip or redesign with poll(POLLOUT)+timeout.

Cross-cutting adjustments: (a) the esde_running guard must be placed per-consumer and skipped under --dry-run/--check (steam-fetch-metadata reads --dry-run at :196) — a blanket main()-top guard would wrongly block legitimate dry runs; (b) atomic_write_text should add shutil.copystat to preserve target perms before os.replace (matters for the global retroarch.cfg) and its backup_once_suffix is NOT applicable to cemu_cfg, which already uses a directory-based .router-backup — route cemu through the atomic-write part only.

Verification/gating: all C++ fixes (W7.0/W6.0, W4.0, W7.1, W2.0, W1.2, W4.1, W5.1, W6.2, W7.2, CC.0, CC.4, W0.0, W0.2, W0.3) gate on a rebuild from ~/esde-build and installation as ES-DE-MAD.AppImage; several need on-device verification with a display (W7.0/W6.0 no-games crash, W7.1 launch-screen input block, W7.2 gamescope-restart survival) — none can be reproduced headless here. The verify-first items are W1.0 (reproduce the wedge with daemon-killed-mid-capture, modal already gone) and W0.1 (confirm any UI stall is even observable before touching the blocking write). plan.executive_summary:
I read REVIEW-FINDINGS.md end-to-end and opened the actual code at every load-bearing file:line across all four proposed batches. The findings are largely accurate, with several important corrections that change sequencing:

WHAT I CONFIRMED IN CODE:
- C++ (Batch A): W7.0/W6.0 real (main.cpp:557/561 call ViewController::start/pauseViewVideos, which deref mCurrentView with NO null guard at ViewController.h:87/89, while the sibling resetViewVideosTimer at :91-95 DOES guard). W4.0/CC.2 real (GuiMadPageSplash.cpp:181 captures raw `sc=switchComp.get()` and derefs it at :183/:195/:203 inside an async response; rebuildList at :90-92 destroys the owning row — the Backends page at :510 already solved this with std::weak_ptr<MadChipRow>). W7.1 real (main.cpp:549 writes setBlockInput(!esHasFocus) UNCONDITIONALLY every frame, before SDL_PollEvent).
- Python data-safety (Batch B): the three helper-source patterns exist exactly as described — steam-collection-sync.py:83-86 (ES-DE pgrep guard), retroarch_cfg.py:213-217 + localpolicy.py:88-98 (tmp+os.replace atomic write with cleanup), steam-collection-sync.py:140-156 (timestamped _TMP move + RECOVERY.txt). All target findings (gamelist writers with no guard, non-atomic cfg writers, bare unlink of user media) are real.
- Update-recovery (Batch C): C0.0/C1.1 confirmed (deck-post-update.sh:129 calls samba-setup.sh with NO sudo; samba-setup.sh:16 hard-exits unless EUID==0). C0.1 confirmed (samba-setup.sh:22 disables readonly, never re-enables). C1.0 confirmed AND I verified the readonly chain: sinden-reinstall-deps.sh:80 re-enables readonly after step 2, so by step 7 (deck-post-update.sh:200) the bare `sudo pacman -S` runs against a read-only root and fails. install.sh:146-151 is the correct reusable disable/keyring/pacman/enable pattern.
- Daemon (Batch D): I confirmed the concurrency root the review's own seam conclusion got WRONG. REVIEW-FINDINGS.md line 17 declared 0.2/10.11 DEAD on a "single stdin thread" premise. That premise is false: device_cmds.py:128-144 _WatchStream runs _scan()->enumerate_devices() on its OWN thread every 2s, and rpc.py:24/82-83 runs slow methods on a 4-worker pool. devices.py:163/193/216-217 mutate the module-global _ENUM_CACHE with NO lock (the file has _SDL_LOCK at :394 as precedent). So 0.2/10.11 is a LIVE race. 2.1 also confirmed (set_ra_option slow=True at systems_cmds.py:242 does an unlocked read-modify-write; making it fast serializes it on the stdin thread). 0.0 confirmed with nuance: the setsid backstop is ALREADY protected (quit-combo-watcher.py:113 trap '' TERM), but the watcher self-SIGTERMs at :120 and the policy's intended FAST 2s escalation is lost.

KEY CORRECTIONS (honesty):
1. Finding 8.0 is DEAD CODE, not a bug to fix. es_systems.xml:8 invokes model2-m2emu.sh (which already correctly uses "$INI" at :28-29), NOT model-2-emulator.sh. The broken `"M2CONFIGFILE"` literal sed lives only in the unused EmuDeck-stock leftover. The REAL live-path crosshair work is finding N3.0 (guard model2-m2emu.sh's sed with `[ -f "$INI" ]`). Do NOT spend effort editing model-2-emulator.sh except to move it to _TMP as cleanup.
2. W1.0 and W0.1 are NOT ready fixes (the adversarial critics are right). W1.0's premise is wrong — onBackendReady() at GuiMadPanel.cpp:163 already force-clears mInputLocked on daemon restart, and the panel's update() doesn't run while a capture modal is topmost, so the proposed client-side reset can mis-fire and leak input to views. W0.1's non-blocking-write fix would CORRUPT the NDJSON wire protocol mid-line (the writeLine partial-write loop). Both are verify-first / likely-skip.
3. N0.0's _mad_sinden flag is provably dead (set at router-config-gui.py:283, never read), but the user-visible symptom (gun drives stray nav) needs on-device confirmation before investing in the honor-the-flag fix.

PLAN SHAPE: 9 sessions. First a tiny helper-module session (fsutil.py + proc_guard.py) so every Python batch can import them. Then the lowest-risk highest-value gamelist guards (pure aborts). Then the atomic-write conversions. Then the C++ rebuild batch (one rebuild boundary). Then update-recovery. Then daemon concurrency. Verify-first leads (W1.0, W7.1 interaction, C0.x chain, N0.0, 8.0-vs-N3.0) are sequenced before or folded into their fixes.

plan.risk_matrix:
HIGH RISK (behavior-altering; test carefully):
- W7.1 (main.cpp:549, order 6): changing setBlockInput from per-frame to transition-only could re-introduce input-leaking-behind-the-Steam-overlay if the focus-LOSS path stops blocking. TEST: on-device, (a) launch-screen countdown no longer dismissed by a stray press, (b) bringing up the Steam overlay still blocks nav, (c) returning still unblocks. Verify-first on the symptom before editing.
- W4.0/CC.2 (GuiMadPageSplash.cpp, order 5): weak_ptr conversion must lock() and bail in BOTH the success and failure lambda branches; missing one still derefs a freed switch. TEST: build + on-device (ideally ASAN) — toggle a splash-pool image then cycle MODE/FIT before the response lands; confirm no crash.
- FIX-C1.0 (deck-post-update.sh:200, order 7): wrong steamos-readonly balance silently breaks a LATER step (set -uo pipefail, no set -e). TEST: read the full 9-step readonly state machine after editing; confirm disable/enable pairs nest correctly and don't leave root writable or break step 8/9.
- 0.0 (quit-combo-watcher.py:120, order 8): detaching quit_cmd could regress the normal clean-quit path. TEST: confirm the watcher exits and the emulator dies on a clean quit; confirm Eden's fast 2s escalation now fires (was taking the 6s backstop).
- 4.0/N0.1 + N2.0 (router-config-gui.py quit/SIGTERM, order 9): calling _clear() at quit-top or adding signal handlers could double-teardown or interact with Tk's mainloop teardown. TEST: on-device — quit from the camera page (no orphaned ffmpeg, Sinden alive), and ES-DE-kills-MAD (cleanup runs).
- N6.0/3.3 (tester_cmds.py:826-839, order 9): escape-combo rework needs the physical X-Arcade; getting the raw-BTN_START tracking wrong could make the only exit from an input-locked test unreachable. TEST: on-cabinet, after re-calibrating Start.
- 5.0/10.7 cemu XML escape (cemu_cfg.py:130-131, order 3): the injection fix (function replacement + xml_escape) must produce byte-identical output for normal device names or it changes every Wii U controller profile. TEST: headless — feed a normal name and a name with &/</>/backslash; assert valid XML out.

MODERATE RISK:
- Atomic-write conversions (order 3): mechanism change; risk is a subtle path/encoding mismatch. TEST headless: each writer produces identical bytes vs the old write for a normal input; tmp file lands in the same dir (same filesystem, so os.replace is atomic).
- recoverable_delete + 6.5 merge semantics (order 4): 6.5 changes copytree merge to replace — a BEHAVIOR change that could surprise; surface it to the user rather than silently flipping. recoverable_delete tmp_base must be on the same filesystem (SD vs /home) or it becomes a slow cross-device copy.
- C0.0/C1.1 samba elevation (order 7): if samba-setup.sh is made self-elevating, ensure it doesn't double-prompt when already called with sudo.

LOW RISK (mechanical / additive):
- Gamelist guards (order 2): pure abort when ES-DE is up; behavior-identical when closed.
- C++ W7.0/W6.0/W2.0/W1.2/W4.1/W5.1/W6.2/W0.0/W0.2/W0.3/CC.0/CC.4/W7.2 (order 5): all local null/range/exception guards matching existing idioms; the only real gate is that the rebuild compiles+links.
- 0.2/10.11 lock (order 8): adding a lock around cache access is additive; only risk is holding it too long (hold around the whole enumerate body is fine — it's a <few-hundred-ms walk, not held across I/O waits).
- 2.1 drop slow=True (order 8): tiny write moves to stdin thread; low risk.
- Regex .group guards, pipefail, context managers (order 9): pure robustness additions.

DO-NOT-IMPLEMENT (critics correct):
- W1.0: the proposed captureModalActive()/client-side reset is built on a false premise and could clear a legitimately-held lock. SKIP unless on-device proves a real residual wedge.
- W0.1: non-blocking write with the partial-write loop would send a partial NDJSON line and desync the protocol. SKIP (or poll(POLLOUT)-timeout redesign only if a real stall is observed).
- W6.1: superseded by CC.0; low value.
